Moses tried to unite the children of Israel by sound laws.

First institute of Moses was the traveling Temple.  First institute of a disciple of Christ was a Temple.  Importance of Mashriqu'l-Adhkar will become evident.
The question of the good or evil of things is determined by reason or by law.  Jews believed the commandments of the Pentateuch to be obligatory--they regard them as matters of law not of reason.
Unimportant conditions underwent transformation but essential teachings were revoiced by Jesus Christ.
These laws were applicable to the period, in the time of Christ not and therefore abrogated.  Through truth all will be united.
His Holiness Christ changed laws.  Laws have to be changed according to the needs and conditions of the times.
Social laws are subject to change according to the exigencies of the time, place and conditions.  Manifestations change social laws.
None do I find faithful unto the Covenant...pride and rebellion have effaced it from the hearts...

Moses was the friend of God.  He affirms the principles, the spirit, the reality of each religion...

The Sun of Reality shone from the Messianic mirror through the wonderful channel of Jesus Christ.  The Jews are holding to the Mosaic mirror, bereft of witnessing the lights in Jesus.

Similarly the Jews were believers in His Holiness Moses, awaiting the coming of the Messiah.  The virtues and perfections of Moses became apparent in His Holiness Jesus Christ most effulgently but the Jews held to the name of Moses, not adoring the virts
Two thousand years ago the Israelites expected a Messiah but when Christ appeared they turned away.
In the Bible the Jews have the promise of the Lord of Hosts and the Messiah.  All peoples awaiting the Two Manifestations.
Jews kept away from the Light of Christ though He was the greatest friend of Moses and made Moses great in the eyes of the world.
Jews were awaiting the Manifestation of Christ...when He came, the Jews rejected Him.  The same is the case of the Manifestation of Baha'u'llah.
The Jews were expecting the appearance of the Messiah but because of imitations they did not believe in Christ when He appeared.

Moses foretold of Jesus Christ.  The foundations of all religious systems all proceed from God.
The Jews prayed for the Messiah to come but because of literal interpretations they were veiled from Him.

Everyone of the divine religions contains essential ordinances which are not subject to change, and material ordinances which are abrogated according to the exigencies of time.  Christ abrogated the commands of Moses.
The Jews believed the Messiah would promulgate all the laws of Moses; when Jesus abrogated the law of divorce the people rose in protest against Him.
Divorce had become so universal that there remained no fixed laws of marriage, therefore Jesus Christ forbade divorce.

lippitt.ref: Gleanings from the Writings of Baha'u'llah, p. 20
lippitt.note: Jesus Christ annulled the law of divorce.  The Jews are still waiting for the Manifestation.
In the time of Moses this was necessary.  Jesus annulled the law of divorce.

Christ proclaimed and completed the laws of Moses.  Becuase of the need and conditions of the times laws were changed.  Because of their adherence to imitations of ancient forms, they rejected Jesus Christ.
In the time of Moses divorce was comformable to the needs and conditions.  In the time of Christ they were not suitable, so Jesus abrogated them.
These outer laws are subject to change according to the exigencies of time, place and conditions.
According to the exigencies of the time of Moses divorce was permissible.  During the time of Christ it was necessary to abrogate Moses' law of divorce.
In the days of Christ conditions had changed; Mosaic law was not adapted to needs therefore it was abrogated.

This is the blessing wherewith Moses blessed the children of Israel
These saints would be manifested in every nation in the world.  They are ready to recognize the Bab, one was Quratu'l-'Ayn
Baha'i writings attest that this refers to Baha'u'llah.

lippitt.note: Moses viewed the Promised Land but died before it was reached.
An analogy of the Revelation in the "Land of Mystery" to the Revelation of Moses which indentified the Holy Land which passed out of the hands of the children of Israel.
Baha'u'llah forced to land on the shores of the Holy Land which was sanctified by the religion of Moses.

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 52
lippitt.note: Promised Land is a spiritual state.  Moses viewed the Promised Land but died before he reached it.  Joshua became Israel's leader.
The prophethood of Muhammad was the same as that of Moses and the power of 'Ali was the same as that of Joshua.
Holy Land honored by the lives of the patriarchs was occupied by Baha'u'llah.

Before the time of the kings, Israel was ruled by judges.  "The Holy Land was honored by the lives and labors of the Hebrew patriarchs, judges, kings..." Fulfilment of prophecy when Baha'u'llah occupied the Holy Land.
"Holy Land" promised to Abraham and his progeny by God, sanctified by Revelation of Moses, honored by the lives and labors of the Hebrew patriarchs, judges, kings and prophets."
Holy Land honored by the lives of the prophets, occupied by Baha'u'llah.
Holy Land honored by the lives and labors of kings, occupied by Baha'u'llah.
Throne of David an eternal kingdom.  Christ re-established it, it had been forgotten.
The Promised One was to be seated on the throne of David, whereas Jesus Christ had no home.  Literal interpretation veiled the reality of Jesus Christ.
The Jews expected that the Messiah would sit on a throne.  Their objections based on ancestral teaching.  They rejected Jesus Christ.
The Jews rejected Jesus Christ because of their literal interpretation of the scriptures.

lippitt.ref: Notes of Mrs. Hoagg to Mrs. Brittingham (*)
lippitt.note: They said that the Messiah should establish a great kingdom--and Jesus came as the poorest of men...the clergy were very busy creating doubts in the minds of people. (* written from Haifa, 1/1914, translated by Monever Khanum)

lippitt.ref: Daily lessons received at Acca, p. 17
lippitt.note: The Mashrak-el-Azkar, to have it built was most important...will have great effect...
Turn thou thy face to the church of God which consists in divine instructions and merciful exhortations...the exhortations of the Beauty of Abha will baptize the soul.
The Messiah was to be seated on the throne of David but Jesus had not even a mat on which to sit.  Literal interpretation veiled the reality of Jesus, the Christ.
His throne and kingdom were established in hearts where He reigns with authority.  The Jews rejected Jesus because of literal and ancestral teachings.
Moses was a simple shepherd...through his influence a whole nation which had been in a state of demoralization, reached a very enlightened civilization.
they rose to such a zenith that the splendor of the new sovereignty of Solomon was made possible, Moses brought unity to Israel--their religion was the quickening life of the body-politic.
Moses gathered the wandering children of Israel and led them as a shepherd to political and religious unity.  Moses, a divine shepherd.

lippitt.ref: The Principle of Religious Unity, p. 2
lippitt.note: Even Greek philosophers journeyed to Jersulem to sit at the feet of the Hebrew sages...
It is evident therefore that Moses was an educator and teacher, culminating in the great power of Solomon's reign.
Moses civilized the children of Israel, brought them from bondage to the powerful nationhood during Solomon's reign.

Moses through the power of religion led Israel from bondage to a great renowned civilization and the splendor of Solomon's reign...Through the divine benediction the Holy Land was conquered and the glory of the Solomonic sovereignty dawned.
In all lines of advancement they achieved distinction.  The Law of [Moses], the Word became the foundation of the laws of nations.
Moses disciplined the children of Israel and laid the foundations of nationhood and the glory of Solomon's reign.
Moses appeared with heavenly radiance and saved the children of Israel.  Moses' leadership culminated in the nationhood of Israel and in the glory of Solomon's reign.
As the result of the institutions that Moses gave them they attained a position which entitled them to honor among all nations and their fame spread to all lands.

Even the Greek philosophers journeyed to Jerusalem to acquire the basis of Israelitish law.  Though but a shepherd Moses, through the power of religious training, rescued Israel from bondage and founded a great civilization.
Moses trained the children of Israel and led them from bondage.  Even the philosophers of Greece went to Palestine to sit at the feet of sages...
The tribes of Israel were torn to pieces, their religion was debased and Israel's glory declined.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IX:3, p. 27

Mt. Carmel, the home of Elijah, on which Baha'u'llah pitched His tent.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:16, p. 270 (*)

Nebuchadnezzar had come burning the context of the whole Bible and taking into captivity many Jewish tribes...the laws and the Torah were forgotten; the foundation of religion and faith was shaken.
Afterwards the prophets reformed Israel and re-established the law of God...
When Israel forsook the foundations of the Law of God Babylon's king conquered the Holy Land.

lippitt.note: The Mashrak-el-Azkar is the most important matter and the greatest divine Institute.  Solomon built the first real Temple.
Throne of David an eternal kingdom, Christ re-established it, it had been forgotten.
His throne and kingdom were established in hearts where he reigns with power.  They rejected Jesus because of literal and ancestral teachings.
This resulted in the liberation of Israel.  Jerusalem and the Temple of Solomon were rebuilt and the glory of Israel restored.

This means that He would wield a sword.  It was the sword of His tongue that separated the right from wrong.  Jews were blind to spiritual symbolism.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:17, p. 172

David sang in His Psalms of Baha'u'llah.
As the day follows night...so Jesus Christ appeared like a "Sun of Truth"...now a great light, a Persian star is spreading the light.
David acclaimed Baha'u'llah the Lord of Hosts and King of Glory.
Baha'u'llah assured us that such a consummation had been prophecied.

Prophecy of the return of the Jews to the Holy Land.  All Palestine to be their home.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIX:2, p. 40
lippitt.note: Universal peace will raise its tent in the center of the earth; the blessed tree of life will overshadow the east and west...will act with love, justice and equity.

Revolutionary changes enabled the outcasts of Israel to be brought back and assemble in the Holy Land.

Men must bestir themselves in this matter and no longer delay in altering conditions which bring grinding poverty to a very large number of people...The Government should conform to the Divine Law which gives equal justice to all

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VIII:19, p. 241 (*)
lippitt.note: Rest assured in the protection of God.  He will preserve His cown children under all circumstances.  He holds the scepter of power in His hand. (* Talk on protection from tests 6/5/1914)

God had revealed to some of His prophets that the Lord of Hosts would be manifested in the Holy Land, fulfilled by Baha'u'llah
On Mt. Carmel Baha'u'llah pitched His tent.
The promises of God, as recorded in the Holy Scriptures have all been fulfilled.
Isaiah alludes to Baha'u'llah in this verse.
The purpose of prophetic words was the inner significance--the Jews were veiled from the truth, therefore they denied Jesus.

lippitt.ref: The Unfoldment of World Civilization, p. 45
lippitt.note: Isaiah and Baha'u'llah make the same prediction.  The Hand of Divine power has laid the foundations of this Holy Cycle.

lippitt.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 46

lippitt.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 205

No man that meditateth upon this verse can fail to recognize the greatness of this Cause...the exalted character of this Day, the Day of God Himself.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf,p.146;Gleanings,13
lippitt.note: Man is admonished to be attentive to God's Word in order to remain steadfast.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 146
lippitt.note: Every fair-minded person is led by the fragrance of these words unto the garden of understanding.
This is the day which the Pen of the Most High hath glorified in all the...Scriptures.  Every man must arise to disseminate the verities of the Cause.
This is the Day glorified in all the Scriptures.
Baha'u'llah's tent on Mt. Carmel.
Tabernacle first erected in Baghdad symbolized the Law of Baha'u'llah.
Mt. Carmel on which Baha'u'llah pitched His tent.
In all Scriptures the sentence of want of understanding hath been pronounced against those whose hearts have been deprived of the Holy Spirit.

lippitt.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose-Garden at Acca, p. 37
lippitt.note: The call of the Kingdom regenerates some; but others are like those Christ mentioned who do not comprehend.
These spiritual dead are unable to understand or perceive the truth.

His Holiness Jesus Christ referring to the prophecy of Isaiah spoke of those having eyes see not, having ears hear not, having hearts, understand not...it is evident that the bounties of Christ transformed eyes, ears, heart.
Bounties of Christ transformed eye, ear and made the heart tender.  We must investigate the foundation of divine religion, spread its message.

lippitt.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 18
lippitt.note: Due to spiritual deadness they do not understand.  Miracles of healing are spiritual, not physical.
In the Kitab-i-Iqan Baha'u'llah upholds the purity and innocence of the Virgin Mary.
Were We to make mention of all that has been revealed in the heavenly Books and holy Scriptures concerning this Revelation, this Tablet would assume impossible dimensions.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 57
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah's testimony to His fulfilment of this prophecy.
Baha'u'llah brought the plan for world peace.
Baha'u'llah referred to by Isaiah as the Counsellor.
Prophecy of the coming of Baha'u'llah in the station of the Father.
Baha'u'llah addressed the Czar, He announced the advent of the promised Father.
Baha'u'llah identifies Himself with the prophecy of the Father's coming.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 18
lippitt.note: 'Abdu'l-Baha bears a burden of griefs and troubles, yet helps all and is happy for He has cut Himself from the world.

lippitt.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 105
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah asserts, "I am the One whom the tongue of Isaiah hath extolled, with Whose name both the Torah and Evangel are adorned.
Isaiah referred to Baha'u'llah as the Branch of Jesse who shall be established upon the throne of David.
Jesus did not fulfill this prophecy, so the Jews rejected him.
People failed to see the Temple of Manifestation.  Literal interpretation hindered--they did not understand symbology.
According to the Jews Jesus Christ did not fulfill this prophecy.  They did not understand the Scriptures...of the life-giving spirit they understood not a word.

lippitt.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 205
lippitt.note: Prophecies fulfilled by Baha'u'llah.
Baha'u'llah referred to by Isaiah as the rod.

This rod might be correctly applied to Christ, for Christ as a descendant of Jesse, the father of David but Christ found existence through the Spirit of God.  He called Himself the Son of God.  If He had not done so, this description would refer to Him.

lippitt.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 205
lippitt.note: No less enthralling is the vision of Isaiah, greatest of the Hebrew prophets, predicting the destiny of mankind.
Christ has addressed the world saying, Except ye become as little children ye shall in no wise enter the kingdom, that is, men must become pure in heart to know God.
They rejected Jesus Christ, Jews did not see these conditions fulfilled.  Roman tyranny enveloped the world and they crucified Christ.

According to the Jews, Jesus Christ fulfilled none of these conditions, for their eyes were holden and they could not see...Symbolic of the unity of mankind which Baha'u'llah brought.  Jesus Christ did not bring peace, therefore Jews rejected him.

This is explained by Isaiah (VI:9), Hear ye indeed but understand not; and see ye indeed but perceive not.  Dead if their hearts have been deprived of the Holy Spirit.

Without the protection and quickening of the Holy Spirit the human world would be extinguished.
Souls alive with animal and rational soul...Christ declared that they are dead in that they were devoid of the spirit of Faith.  Spiritual deadness, bestowal of the Holy Spirit.
The alive are those souls in whom the spirit of life is breathed from the Presence of God...all besides them are dead.

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons delivered at Acca, p. 42

If the souls of humanity are living and yet have not received a portion of the spirit they are as dead.
Souls deprived of the divine spirit accounted as dead.
Body in need of physical and mental energy but his spirit requires fortification of the Holy Spirit.
Spiritually dead because they did not believe in Christ, only physically alive.
Without illumination from God the physically alive would be spiritually dead.  Were it not for the love of God the pathway of the Kingdom would be closed.
The human soul is in need of divine vivification emanating from the Holy Spirit, without it man would be an animal.
It is important to give perception and eternal life, that is, the spiritual and divine life; for physical life is not immortal and its existence is equivalent to non-existence.
Physical life in comparison with the life in the Kingdom of God is considered as death.
Those heedless of God are dead compared with the people of faith...

souls who are not vivified by the Holy Spirit are accounted among the dead.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17

lippitt.note: The Jews said the Messiah was to spread the law of Moses but Jesus seeks to destroy it.  They were blind to Reality.  They were deprived of the Beauty of Christ.

Because they were blindly following imitations the Jews rejected Jesus, they refused to investigate the truth.
Manifestations changed some of the laws of the Jews--which they called the law of Moses.  Altered according to the exigencies of the times.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 241(Tablet to MacNutt)
lippitt.note: They declared that Christ was a breaker of the Sabbath.  Although on the surface they seemed to defend the Cause of God, yet inwardly their purpose was to manifest error.  Violators flatter, seduce and show insincere love.
He broke the Sabbath Day.  He reformed the law of Moses, therefore the Jews rejected Him.
Because Jesus dared to rebuke the Pharisees the Jews rejected Him.  The Jews knew by heart the letter, of the Spirit they did not understand, they did not understand the Scriptures.
The Jews believed the Messiah would promulgate all the laws of Moses; when Jesus abrogated the laws of the Sabbath, the people rose in protest against Him.
Jews were veiled from the truth by literal interpretation; therefore they rejected Jesus Christ.
Manifestations change the material laws in conformance with needs and conditions of the times.  Jews were veiled from reality by ancestral traditions.

lippitt.ref: Wisdom of 'Abdu'l-Baha, p. 96; Paris Talks, p. 105
lippitt.note: Thou Jesus abrogated the Law of Moses, it did not prevent the spread of the Gospel.  The Pharisees said of Christ that He had broken the Sabbath.

Because Jesus Christ did not observe the Sabbath according to Scriptures, they rejected Him.  Literal interpretation and narrow practice led them to reject Jesus Christ.
Because of the needs and conditions of the time laws were changed.  The Jews were unwilling to change from old forms and teachings.
Jesus Christ changed the laws regarding the Sabbath because of the needs and conditions of the time.
In the days of Christ conditions had changed, Mosaic Law not adapted to the needs, therefore Christ abrogated the law of the Sabbath.

lippitt.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 31
lippitt.note: The Sabbath of the Jews was changed by Christ.  The outward rules may be changed but the reality is unchanging.

In His day Christ was called Satan, Beelzebub but hear the bells now ringing for Him!  Although men rise against the Kingdom, the dominion and sovereignty of God will be set up.

lippitt.note: The Jews believed the Messiah would promulgate all the laws of Moses; when Jesus abrogated the law of divorce the people rose in protest against Him.

Because of the needs and conditions of the times, laws were changed.  Because of their adherence to imitation of ancient forms, the Jews rejected Jesus.
outer laws are subject to change according to the exigencies of time, place and conditions.  Manifestations make such changes.
Jesus Christ abrogated this law because the exigency which existed in the time of Moses did not exist.

[God] appropriateth by His mercy those whom He liketh and chooseth for His love those of whom He approveth.

the chosen: upon whom He caused evidensed Light to descend...guided into the right Path, and to whom He gave the Glad-tidings of the Great Success!...Ye must be sincere and faithful, ye must follow the ordinances which refer to the Covenant...
This is a station which God giveth to whomsoever He pleaseth.

Jews rose against Jesus but He is now glorified; in spite of the hatred of men God's dominion will be set up, eternal.

may you offer yourselves in the threshold of sacrifice for the betterment of the world.

He arose to teach and educate men and so He sacrificed Himself to give the spirit of life.  He perished in body so as to quicken others by His spirit.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:1, p. 14 (*)
lippitt.note: Remember, the most learned doctors of His country in His age condemned Him to be killed, whilst one who was a catcher of fish believed in Him. (*Lawh-i-Aqdas, Baha'u'llah)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:2, p. 40 (*)
lippitt.note: He sacrificed His life that the oil in the lamps of our souls might become ignited and His Light shine forth from them. (*Address in New York, 6/11/1912)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, V:11, p. 169
lippitt.note: They crucified their promised Messiah...

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XV:9, p. 254 (*)
lippitt.note: Jesus Christ gave His life on the cross for the unity of mankind.  Only a few were attracted to Him but with this small army He conquered the world. (*Address in New York)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 633 (*)
lippitt.note: Some Jews cried, O God, hasten the Day when the promised One will manifest Himself.  When He came they knew Him not...killed Him...they could not see the Reality of Christ. (*Bahai Scriptures, p. 729-30)

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 31
lippitt.note: All Manifestations have been denied and persecuted; men have worshipped the personality but did not recognize the Reality, the Truth.

lippitt.ref: Utterances in letter from Mrs. Hoagg to Mrs. (*)
lippitt.note: The Jewish clergy were busy creating doubts in the minds of people...Therefore they say Jesus was a false Messiah... (Brittingham, Haifa, 1/1914, translated Monever Khanum)
The crucifixion was the means of spreading the light of the Cause of God.
From Mary Magdalene Jesus cast out seven devils...observe her transformation and glory. (*Talk in 'Akka, 11/10/1908)

lippitt.ref: Star of the west, X:12, p. 232
lippitt.note: From Mary Magdalene Jesus cast out seven devils...observe her transformation and glory after she believed in Christ.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:17, p. 307 (*)
lippitt.note: Later on it became evident what crowns studded with brilliant jewels of guidance were placed on the heads of Mary Magdalene and the 'other Mary', the Mother of Jesus. (*Unveiling the Divine Plan for the Islands of the Sea)

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 62
lippitt.note: Because it is clear literal text, it is believed...Explanation of symbolism.
Mary Magdalene was transformed and became the means through which the confirmation of God descended.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
lippitt.note: Mary Magdalene held fastly to the Christ and made others firm.  God will assist all who are firm in His Cause.
Mary Magdalene strengthened the disciples.  In the sight of God sex makes no difference.  He or she is greatest who is nearest God.
Mary Magdalene was superior to all the men of her time.  She gathered the others together and said, Why do y mourn?  Her reality is shining from the horizon of Christ.
Mary Magdalene kept firm in the Cause; she surpassed men in depending the Faith against suspicions.  This is a manifest light.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
lippitt.note: Mary Magdalene held fastly to the Christ and made others firm.  God will assist all who are firm in the Cause.
Christ was a receiver of inspiration; but what a difference between the inspiration of the Word of God and the revelations of Isaiah, Jeremiah and Elijah!
Jesus lighted the torch...after Him the Apostles went far carrying the Gospel.
Consider the influence of Jesus Christ on His Apostles.  These simple men were enabled by the Power of the Holy Spirit to spread the Glad Tidings.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:2, p. 41 (*)
lippitt.note: We must be willing to give our lives...we must go among the peoples of the world...summoning them. (*Address at 309 W. 78th St, New York)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, V:1, p. 6
lippitt.note: Command to travel in every country...as the Apostles of Christ did.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:10, p. 85 (*)
lippitt.note: Through this superhuman service the rays of peace and conciliation may illumine and enlighten all the regions, and the world of humanity may find peace. (*Tablet to the West)
Among [Jesus's) principles of spiritual civilization He came to proclaim is the Most Great Peace of mankind--the Oneness of the kingdom of humanity.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:15, p. 282 (*)
lippitt.note: Be severed from the physical world, become angels of heaven and travel and teach through all these regions...each one shall blow the breath of life in the souls of others. (*Unveiling the Divine Plan for the Western World)

lippitt.note: Such must be the purity of character that people may be drawn closer to the Heavenly Father, the Source of purity and grace.
The pure in heart shall see with the eyes of the Spirit and shall recognize God in His great Manifestation, Baha'u'llah.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
lippitt.note: Be of the Spirit, not of the body.  The Light of the body is the eye.  Spirtuality is the possession of a good pure heart.
The Bab's admonition to His disciples to ponder the words of Jesus as He sent out His disciples.
oneness of God and of mankind...this is the bond of union among Baha'is all over the world.
the friends of God are the salt of the earth.  [They] have been endowed with the ability and capacity on which account they became the "chosen" and not the "called".
Be upon your guard in order that none may assail the oneness and integrity of Baha'u'llha's Cause.
All spiritual prophecies concerning the coming of Christ were fulfilled but the Divine Reality of Jesus was unrecognized.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:3, p. 54 (*)
lippitt.note: Rebuke of Christ to the Jews.  He promulgated the ten commandments. (*Answers to theologians in Paris)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:17, p. 173 (*)
lippitt.note: The character of divine sovereignty has no change or transformation, but the organization and administration change continually.  Two kinds of divine teachings, the spiritual and the material. (*Article, International Psychic Gazette)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 633 (*)
lippitt.note: When Jesus Christ appeared among the Jews, the first thing He did was to proclaim the validity of the Mosaic Mission.  He declared that the Torah, the Old Testament, was the Word of God. (*Bahai Scriptures, p. 729-30)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 636 (*)
lippitt.note: The foundation of the reality of the law of Moses is that of Christianity.  Had it not been for Christ you would not have heard the name of Moses. (*Bahai Scriptures, p. 333)
Jesus Christ renewed the Commandments.
Jesus spread the name of Moses throughout the Orient and the Occident.  We must investigate reality...not accept ancestral imitations or hearsay.
Jesus renewed the commandments which Moses had received from Jehovah. He said the Commandments in the Pentateuch were to be followed and He insisted they must be maintained.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 77
lippitt.note: Christ taught obedience.  Muhammad taught resignation.  Baha'u'llah taught renunciation.
This was according to the spirit of the [Mosaic] age, but as these laws were not expedient in the time of Christ, they were abrogated.
The stern old law changed into one of love by Manifestations.

lippitt.ref: The Principles of Religious Unity, p. 4
lippitt.note: Jesus Christ abrogated laws because conditions had changed requiring the laws.

Every one of the divine religions contains essential ordinances which are not subject to change, and material ordinances which are abrogated according to the exigencies of time.  Christ abrogated the commands of Moses...
These laws were applicable to the period, but in the time of Jesus not applicable...therefore abrogated.  Through truth all would be united and agreed.
His Holiness Christ changed laws...non-essentials are changed according to the exigencies of the time and conditions.
Social laws changed in accordance with the exigencies of the time, place and conditions.  Manifestations changed such laws.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VIII:4, p. 47 (*)
lippitt.note: Different teachings are needed for different people. (*Talk with Mrs. Dreyfus-Barney)
When we hold fast to the foundation of religion, difference will disappear.  We should not resist evil doers.
allegiance to the Word of God alone establishes unity and love.
Explanation of justice and mercy of God.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:3, p. 54 (*)
lippitt.note: No connection between the holy teachings and war. (*Answers to theologians in Paris)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, V:8, p. 116
lippitt.note: Remarks about disarmament and universal peace.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VI, p. 61
lippitt.note: All religions have two departments, spiritual and material...investigation of truth.  Religion is one.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:6, p. 104
lippitt.note: Welcome persecution, hostility and bitterness as the means of testing and increasing your supreme faith in God.
There is need of turning back to the original foundation.  Baha'u'llah has re-established the teachings of the Prophets.
Consider how much the principles of the religion of Christ have been forgotten...and many heresies appeared.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:18, p. 179 (DMAS, 5/23/1914)
lippitt.note: Admonitions regarding true brotherhood.  The Baha'is must fulfil to the letter the requirements of these holy principles.
Hatred and persecution cannot be reconciled with Christ's teaching...God's love and care for all mankind.

lippitt.note: The bounty of God is transcendent, unrestricted, universal.
the mercy of God is universal.
Mercy of God encircles all of mankind.  No soul is deprived.

lippitt.ref: World Order through World Faith,  p. 17
lippitt.note: All are sheltered beneath the tree of His providence and mercy--the mercy of God is universal.

lippitt.note: It necessitates the focalization of all heavenly virtues...Be the image and likeness of God.
The world of humanity must...be the recipient of bounties from the eternal.  Man must learn...from God Himself.

lippitt.note: Strive that your ideals may be realized in the Kingdom of God on earth...Use your knowledge so war may cease and the glorious edifice of Peace be raised.

lippitt.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 46
lippitt.note: A world catastrophe must preceed and usher in the Kingdom...as anticipated by Jesus Christ.
Glory of God is eternal...
Reconstruction of the world must take place before the Will of God can be done on earth--signs of awakening since Baha'u'llah's Revelation.
'Abdu'l-Baha made plans to carry Divine Plans for establishing the Kingdom of God in the Western world.
Baha'u'llah is the establisher of the Kingdom which Jesus Christ promised.

browser.central_figures:

link

lippitt.note: That prayer is also a prophecy...depends on your service.
If people of the entire globe are welded in one great commonwealth the prayer "Thy will be done" will be a reality.
This is the time of which Jesus Christ spoke when He gave the Lord's Prayer...I hope that this is your expectation and great desire.
It is the duty of the followers of Baha'u'llah to proclaim the approach of the Christ-promised Kingdom.
Can this be done by human power?  No, in the name of God!  For example, Christ alone...upraised the standard of peace and righteousness.
Some days He ['Abdu'l-Baha] hardly saw His own family, so pressed was He by those who crowded to the Birubi for some kind of help--following the above teaching of Christ.
With the language of the heart man prays to God.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 36
lippitt.note: John the Baptist came before Christ.  Each Manifestation came after a Herald.  Ahmad and Kazim were forerunners before the Manifestation of this Day.
Explanation that return of Elias is not of the personality but the reality of the perfections.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IX:9, p. 103
lippitt.note: Water symbolizes the knowledge of God which gives eternal life because all life had its beginning in water.  Knowledge and purity is water, love is fire...Ceremonies have spiritual meaning...It is not true that if a child dies without the baptism

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:2, p. 38 (*)
lippitt.note: The Spirit is conducive to everlasting glory; it is the cause of the exultation of the world of humanity.  May each one of you become the recipient of the Spirit.  (*Talk in Paris, 2/14/1913)
This Spirit of faith is the flame of reality, the life of humanity and the cause of eternal illumination.
He who is baptized by the Holy Spirit is a Manifestation of divine mercy to mankind.
Christ appeared in order to illumine the world of humanity...to unite hearts...Through Christ they were intended to be baptized by the Holy Spirit, attain a new spirit and realize life everlasting.
This is not a material dove...it was a spiritual state expressed by a figure.  Symbolism abounds in Scriptures.
to complete the law of the former religion He confirmed the past law.  Christ desired this institution to be used at that time.
Two-fold nature of the Manifestations--bodily and Oneness with God.  It had not been revealed unto Him before His baptism that His station was that of the Manifestation of God.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIV:9, p. 274
lippitt.note: The story about the dove is simply a metaphor.  No dove came down...the Holy Spirit was always with Christ.  He knew about His Mission from early childhood.
The Call of Baha'u'llah surpassed the experience of Jesus Christ at His baptism.
The descent of the dove upon Jesus Christ symbolic of the Annunciation to Baha'u'llah of His Mission.
Most Great Spirit designated by Baha'u'llah symbolized in Christian Dispensation as the Dove.
God conferred upon that Essence of the Spirit, Who was known amongst the people as fatherless, the glory of Prophethood, and made Him His testimony unto all that are in heaven and on earth.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 113
lippitt.note: The Sonship of Jesus Christ asserted by Baha'u'llah.
The Sonship station is the heart of Christ and the Holly Spirit is the station of the Spirit of Christ.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 45
lippitt.note: Manifestations are expected but when They come, they are rejected because of the blindness of people.

lippitt.ref: The Advent of Divine Justice, p. 38
lippitt.note: Peter was the first Apostle called.  Observe how this crude and helpless instrument was transformed by the power of the Son Jesus Christ; how much more is that of the Father revealed in Baha'u'llah.
these disciples able to do what philosophers and scientists failed to accomplish through the Holy Spirit.
The two great Apostles, St. Peter and St. John the Evangelist were once simple humble workmen.  By the power of the Holy Spirit their souls were illumined.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 31
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah asked the Pope to consider how the great leader pronounced judgment against Jesus while a lowly fisherman recognized His station, and said, Take heed then, ye men of understanding!
Through the aid and assistance of God they became mighty ones of God.  Peter was transformed.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 6-7 (*)
lippitt.note: Simple men filled with the Holy Spirit influenced humanity to such an extent that thousands would die to uphold their teachings...Peter had no education. He who is educated by the Holy Spirit can educate others (*Discourses in London and Paris)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:3, p. 39 (*Tablet to Agnes)
lippitt.note: The Apostles of Christ were insignificant persons; at present consider what change has taken place.  Do not look at present condition, contemplate the future.  Bounties of Baha'u'llah shall in the future become manifest. (*Parsons,1/8/1919)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:12, p. 232 (*)
lippitt.note: After the baptism of the Holy Spirit, Peter became a great preacher.  This is what is meant by everlasting life, by baptism with the Holy Spirit and by being born again. (*Tablet to Mrs. Annie Parmerton, Cincinnati)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:15, p. 258 (*)
lippitt.note: The Apostles were fishers.  Consider to what a high station they did rise!  Their sovereignty runs to eternity. (*Tablet to Roy C. Williams [Wilhelm?])

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIII:7, p. 173
lippitt.note: Unity and concord is their bait...service and self-sacrifice is their support...Let the Baha'is be the fishermen of the sea of spirituality.  Let them not lose their courage.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 43
lippitt.note: The disciples were humble fishermen...All souls have capacity for enkindlement by the Spirit.
Yahya, John the Baptist was Herald of the Word.
Remarks concerning baptism.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 45
lippitt.note: Elijah came but was not recognized in John...not the entity of Elijah but his qualities that John embodied.
Baha'u'llah gave a greater promise to followers than Jesus to Peter.  He assures them that if they choose to follow Him He will make them become Quickeners of Mankind.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 124
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah asserts that while fishers of men was individual, quickeners of mankind is universal.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IX:10, p. 112 (*Talk, 6/13/1914)
lippitt.note: Many common souls have advanced to the divine Manifestations.  Ignorant people accepted Muhammad.  However, everyone testifies that all the learned philosophers and nobles of every nation were humble in the presence of the Blessed Beauty, and great numb

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 110
lippitt.note: In this day, however, We say, Come ye after Me that We may make you to become quickeners of mankind.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 113
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah asserted that the mystery of the immaculate conception of the Virgin Mary is confessed.
Through Jesus Christ the leper received healing from the leprosy of ignorance.
Forgiveness was granted through Jesus Christ.
This is the real sovereignty and such is God's power in His chosen Ones.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VIII:18, p. 231
lippitt.note: He did not use prayers or mental suggestion.  He used spiritual authority in the command, which was the power of the Holy Spirit.  His Word was creative.
The Jews regarded Jesus as a breaker of the Sabbath...they did not understand the significance, therefore they were debarred from perceiving the truth of Christ.
Because they were blindly following imitations, the Jews rejected Jesus...they refused to investigate the truth.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 239 (*Tablet to Mr.&)
lippitt.note: They declared that Christ was a breaker of the Sabbath.  Although on the surface they seemed to defend the Cause of God, yet inwardly their purpose was to manifest error. Violators flatter, seduce and show insincere love.(*Mrs. MacNutt)

lippitt.ref: Utterances in letter of Mrs. Hoagg to Mrs.(*)
lippitt.note: The Jewish clergy said, the Promised One must promote the laws of the Bible and this man broke the Sabbath.  They created doubts in the minds of the people. (*Brittingham, 1/1914)
The Jews said the Messiah was to spread the law of Moses but Jesus seeks to destroy it.  They were blind to Reality.
Manifestations change the material laws in conformance with the needs and conditions of the times.  Jews were veiled from reality by ancestral traditions.
This was according to the spirit of the age, but as these laws were not expedient in the time of Christ, they were abrogated.
Though Jesus Christ defied the law of Moses, it did not prevent the spread of the Gospel.
Jesus Christ gave the spiritual truth regarding the Sabbath--the Jews were veiled from the truth by narrow literal interpretation.
Jesus Christ changed the laws regarding the Sabbath because of the needs and conditions of the time.
In the days of Christ, conditions had changed--Mosaic law was not adapted to the needs--therefore Christ abrogated the law of the Sabbath.
The Jews believed the Messiah would promulgate all the laws of Moses; when Jesus abrogated the laws of the Sabbath the people rose in protest against Him.

lippitt.ref: Wisdom of 'Abdu'l-Baha, p. 49; Paris Talks, p. 55
lippitt.note: He broke the Sabbath Day.  He reformed the Law of Moses, therefore the Jews rejected Him.
Because Jesus Christ did not observe the Sabbath according to the Scriptures, they rejected Him.  Literal interpretation and narrow practice led them to reject Jesus Christ.
Because of the needs and conditions of the times, laws were changed.  The Jews were unwilling to change from old forms and teachings.
The Jews were veiled from truth by literal interpretation; therefore they rejected Jesus Christ.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 58
lippitt.note: Analogy between the rejection of Jesus Christ and the divines' rejection of Baha'u'llah.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
lippitt.note: Twelve believed in Him; one deserted Him--others were not strong.  God will assist all who are firm in His Cause.
Always with their teacher, the Divine Master, they learned through His teachings.
The product of the life of Christ was the illumined souls of those who believed in Him.

Christ in reality educated only eleven men of whom one thrice denied Him.  Cause of Christ permeated the world.  Baha'u'llah has educated thousands of souls.  Then reflect what will take place in the future.
Consider the days of Christ, how the light of guidance brightened twelve hearts...The lamp of guidance has been lighted in your hearts.
The superstition about the number thirteen originated in the fact that it was the thirteenth disciple who betrayed Jesus.  Superstitions regarding lucky or unlucky numbers are imaginary.
Let them that are men of action follow in their footsteps.
Only eleven began Apostles of Jesus Christ--they illumined the world...similar conditions in Paris.  Little by little the Cause grew.
In each cycle the guardians or holy souls have been twelve.  So in the time of Christ there were twelve Apostles.
Jesus really had only eleven for Judas betrayed Him.
Although men may arise against the Kingdom, God's dominion will be set up.  Truth will prevail.
The Jews rejected Jesus and tortured Him but Christianity advanced daily.
The Pharisees called Jesus Beelzebub.  The deriding enemy addeth joy to the heart--becometh means of spirituality.

lippitt.ref: Utterance in letter of Mrs. Hoagg to Mrs.(*)
lippitt.note: When Jesus came the Jews called Him Beelzebub.  I have always said that the believers will be blamed for this Cause.  The Jews proved from the Bible that Jesus was a false Prophet--It is always so. (*Brittingham, 1/1914, Haifa)
Moses announced the coming of Christ.  The Jews were blind to Reality.  The real Christ Spirit has come again from the supreme apex in Baha'u'llah to illumine the world.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:14, p. 241 (*)
lippitt.note: Cause of Christ had no importance then.  Analogy regarding the Covenant which is not yet known befittingly. (*Tablet to Roy C. Wilhelm)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XXV:?, p. 258
lippitt.note: Christ in His Day was called Satan, today He is as the Word of God.
You are My spiritual children; spiritual children are dearer than physical children--you are most beloved.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 47
lippitt.note: Spiritual relationship is the true familyhood of God's children.
Christ said His brothers were those who believed in God...

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIII:1, p. 24
lippitt.note: This Holiness the Christ said that His brothers were those who believed in God, and refused to associate with His own brothers.  It is impossible for a man to associate with a leper and not be infected.
Attainment is dependent upon capacity.  Unless capacity is developed the summons of the Kingdom cannot reach the ear, the light of the Sun of truth will not be observed...

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 43
lippitt.note: The seed contained the Truth.  Some behold in the seed only a hard substance while others see in it the life principle.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XII:10, p. 182
lippitt.note: Some have no capacity; others hear and understand but forget; others have great capacity and live accordingly.
Symbolic of the seed of life which needs experiences.
In all Scriptures the sentence of want of understanding hath been pronounced against those whose hearts have been deprived of the Holy Spirit.

lippitt.ref: Flower...from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 37 (*)
lippitt.note: The Kingdom is like a spirit--the second birth will be produced, but other souls do not comprehend. (*Tablet, translated 11/12/1908)
Bounties of Christ transformed eye, ear and made the heart tender.  We must investigate the foundation of religion--spread its message.
Be upon your guard in order that none assail the oneness and integrity of Baha'u'llah's Cause.
The disciples of Christ were physically weak and apparently vanquished at first--yet in the end they were victorious.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:13, p. 122 (*)
lippitt.note: Man must live in the utmost sanctity and purity and he must ever think to assist others and not beg assistance from them.  (*Talk in Haifa, 10/15/1914)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:2, p. 34 (*Unveiling the)
lippitt.note: Teachers must travel to all parts of the world like 'Abdu'l-Baha.  He was sanctified and free from every attachment. (*Divine Plan for the World)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 241 (*)
lippitt.note: The Covenant is like unto the ocean which preserves Baha'i unity; and these souls are like unto the foam upon it...I acted in accordance with Christ's admonition. (*Tablet to Mr. & Mrs. MacNutt)
'Abdu'l-Baha's instructions for traveling teachers.
They should seek no reward, freely they should give...utter with eloquence...guide the people to the manifest light, in great joy.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 241 (*)
lippitt.note: I acted in accordance with the admonition of Christ...notwithstanding that unworthy behavior I was still patient...I paid their expenses, showed them the utmost consideration... (*Tablet to Mr. & Mrs. MacNutt)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, V:14, p. 215 (*)
lippitt.note: We must act accordingly and follow in their footsteps. (*Tablet called Trumpet call to the East and West)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XX:10, p. 299
lippitt.note: Those who say they are healing through the power of God should accept no pay, for they are dispensers of God's bounty--channels of His grace.
Explanation of the return of the perfections rather than the person of Elias.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, V:8, p. 122 (*)
lippitt.note: Review of Jewish history, ending with the execution of John the Baptist. (*Talk with rabbi)
The Bab admonished the Letters of the Living to ponder the words of Jesus as He sent them forth to propagate the Cause of God.  He bade them fulfill their mission.
Analogy to the Bab's sending forth of the Letters of the Living...He recalled the words addressed by Jesus to His disciples and emphasized the superlative greatness of the new Day.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:11, p. 106 (*)
lippitt.note: The miracle and symbolism of the loaves and fishes explained. (*Talk with Mrs. von Lilienthal and Mrs. Beede)

lippitt.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 34
lippitt.note: Loaves are the five words of Jesus which satisfied the multitude, the fish represents spiritual meaning, and basketsful the superabundance of this teaching for others to share.
I hope that it is proven to you that Moses had no better friend than His Holiness Christ.
Jesus spread the name of Moses throughout the Orient and Occident.  We must investigate reality--not accept ancestral imitations.
The Jews were rebuked by Jesus for observing the letter and rejecting the Revelation of God.  Jesus extolled Moses.  Manifestations testify to the oneness of the Prophets.
Christ ratified and proclaimed the Law of Moses.  All Prophets have revoiced that same foundation of reality.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:3, p. 54 (*)
lippitt.note: Rebuke of Christ...He promulgated the ten commandments. (*Answers to theologians in Paris)

lippitt.ref: The Principle of Religious Unity, p. 2
lippitt.note: when the Jews became fettered by empty forms and imitations, the wrath of God became manifest.

lippitt.ref: The Principle of Religious Unity, p. 3-4
lippitt.note: Jesus Christ revoiced and confirmed the essential teachings of Moses...ratified and proclaimed the foundation of the law of Moses.
The greatest friend of Moses was Jesus, but the Jews felt He was taking away some of Moses' glory.  They were veiled from the truth and kept away from His bounties.
Jesus renewed the commandments Moses had received from Jehovah.  He said the commandments in the Pentateuch were to be followed and He insisted they must be maintained.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 633 (*)
lippitt.note: When Jesus Christ appeared amongst the Jews, the first thing He did was to proclaim the validity of the Mosaic Mission.  He declared that the Torah, the Old Testament was the Word of God. (*Bahai Scriptures, p. 729)
Jesus rebuked the Jews for their disobediance to the spirit of the commandments--observing requirements only.

lippitt.ref: The Principle of Religious Unity, p. 4
lippitt.note: Jesus Christ proclaimed and ratified the prophethood of Moses.
Jesus Christ declares Moses to have been a Prophet of God.
Christ caused the name and prestige of Moses to become widespread.  Through Christ Moses was proclaimed a Prophet of God.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 103
lippitt.note: The effect of a Manifestation is to drive out all that is evil and replace the natural growth of virtues.

lippitt.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 18
lippitt.note: Because of their spiritual deadness they do not understand.  Miracles were spiritual and not physical.

lippitt.ref: Flowers...from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 27 (*)
lippitt.note: No one is able to follow Christ entirely except he give up all material things, such as honor, wealth... (*Talk by Mirza Haydar 'Ali on 11/11/1908, Acca)
The land of Syria hath been honored by the footsteps of its Lord.  The Scriptures have been fulfilled.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 57
lippitt.note: We have said "He Who is the Father is come...Blessed is he that draweth nigh unto Him and woe betide them that are far away."

lippitt.ref: The Advent of Divine Justice, p. 65
lippitt.note: Every Prophet hath announced the coming of this Day; allusions were made to it in all the sacred Scriptures.

lippitt.ref: Flowers...from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 18 (*)
lippitt.note: All are invited to partake of the fruit of the Kingdom of the Father for He discloses mysteries of the teachings of Baha'u'llah. (*Talk by Ibn Abhar in Acca, 11/10/1908)

lippitt.ref: Flowers...from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 19 (*)
lippitt.note: Jesus Christ did not claim to have come in that station but that He would return in the glory of the Father.  This glory was to be the unity of mankind. (*Talk by Ibn Abhar, Acca, 11/10/1908)

lippitt.ref: Flowers...from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 32(*)
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah is the trunk, the branches, the root--all except the fruit, which is 'Abdu'l-Baha.  The teachings of Baha'u'llah have been spread throughout the world. (*Talk by Ibn Abhar, Acca, 11/12/1908)
Fulfilled in the coming of Baha'u'llah and attested to by His Name.
Jesus referred to Baha'u'llah as the One Who shall come with all His holy angels about Him and all nations gathered before His throne.
In Baghdad the Tabernacle of the Lord of Hosts was first erected and the foundations of the long awaited Kingdom of the Father unassailably established.
Jesus Christ, returned in the glory of the Father, was about to ascend His throne.
Baha'u'llah announced the advent of One with whose name the Evangel was adorned.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 110
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah asserted the clergy interposed between the flock and the returned Christ who came in the glory of the Father.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 61
lippitt.note: These tidings are the same in all the Holy Books expressed in different ways.  Two successive Manifestations, Elias and Christ, the Bab and Baha'u'llah.

lippitt.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 137-38
lippitt.note: 'Abdu'l-Baha is not a Manifestation of God; He is not to be acclaimed the "Return of Christ", the Son Who will come in the glory of His Father.  He reflects even as a clear Mirror the rays of Baha'u'llah's glory...

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 50
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah declared the divines did not know the station of Jesus Christ.
The faithful are ever sustained by the presence of the Supreme Concourse.
This was a spiritual condition expressed as a physical meeting...
God conferred upon that Essence of the Spirit Who was known amongst the people as fatherless, the glory of Prophethood and made Him His testimony unto all that are in heaven and on earth.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 113
lippitt.note: The Sonship of Jesus Christ asserted by Baha'u'llah.
meaning that the Sun is visible and manifest in the Mirror.  Sonship station is the heart of Christ and the Holy Spirit is the station of the Spirit of Christ.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 32]
lippitt.note: The Proof of the Manifestation is the Manifestation Himself.  Jesus was born in a manger but He changed the whole world by His power and Divinity.
If you give the same teachings to all some might be retarded by them instead of helped.
This is as regards its virtues and not as regards identity.  This is our view of reincarnation.

lippitt.note: The humbler a man is in the path of God, the more exalted is he; the greater his meekness and submissiveness, the more beloved is he.  (*Talk to believers and students, 7/1914)
