lippitt.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit at Acca, p. 31
lippitt.note: Jesus Christ taught truth concerning divorce.  He abrogated the law of Moses.  Social laws change according to the needs of the time and conditions.  True religion is always one.  The spirit of religion is unchangeable.
Material laws were changed by the Manifestations according to the needs and conditions of the times.
The Jews believed the Messiah would promulgate all the laws of Moses; when Jesus abrogated the law of divorce, the people rose in protest against Him.
Material laws were changed by the Manifestations according to the needs and conditions of the times.

Law of Moses--divorce--changed because of needs and condition s of the times.  In the time of Jesus this law was the cause of corruption.  Their spiritual blindness led them to adherence to old forms.
The exigency which existed in the time of Moses did not exist, therefore Christ abrogated this law of divorce.
In the days of Christ conditions had changed--Mosaic Law not adapted to the needs--therefore Christ abrogated it.
This was according to the spirit of the age, but as these lawswere not expedient in the time of Christ, they were abrogated.
Jesus Christ annulled the law of divorce.  The Jews still waitng for the Manifestation.
Manifestations change outer laws according to the exigency of time, place and conditions.
In the time of Moses divorce was conformable to the needs and conditions but in the time of Jesus it was the cause of corruptiion, therefore He abrogated that law.
The true Baha'i loves the children.  A simple pure heart is near to God.
Blessed are the children of whom His Holiness Christ said, Of such are the Kingdom of heaven...hearts are pure, we must become like them.
As long as you are the children of the Kingdom, no matter what language you use, it is sweet.
You are the children of whom His Holiness Christ said, Of such is the kingdom of God, and according to the word of Baha'u'llah, you are the lamps of humanity.  You are near the Source.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:9, p. 155 (*)
lippitt.note: children are tender plants, their hearts are clear and transparent. (*Letters recording talks in Stuttgart and Esslingen, Germany)
Daily grow in faith and assurance and develop Baha'i attributes, such as obediance and kindness.

Jesus Christ was instrumental in publishing the name and greatness of the Israelitish prophets, and proved to the world that the Israelites constituted the people of God.  We have stated the valid proofs of the Prophethood of Moses.
The true seeker will gladly renounce worldly desires and possessions if they interfere with spiritual growth.
Earth's happiness does not depend upon wealth.  The rich depend on riches whereas the poor are dependent on God.
Blessed are the poor.  Christ said so.  Baha'u'llah said so.  We must be free from the insatiable desire for riches.  Poor are blessed.
Jesus said, Blessed are the poor. I love you all; you are children of the Kingdom and are accepted of God.
one's heart must not be attached to the things of this world.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IX:9, p. 100 (*)
lippitt.note: Every firm believer will consider himself the servant of all the friends of God...of the world of humanity.  The honor of man depends upon this. (*Talk with students, DMAS, 7/15/1914)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIII:5, p. 104 (*)
lippitt.note: In the path of God humility is honor.  Whoever is the real servant of the believers of God is their helper.  (*Talk given to students on Mt. Carmel)

lippitt.ref: Flowers...from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 27
lippitt.note: Jesus gave His life for mankind.  He proved His own teaching; people believed then obeyed, they found deliverance from sin.
When Christ appeared...He invited men to eternal life, the Light of Guidance was shed forth by that radiant Star and He at length gave His life in sacrifice for humanity.
The Christ sacrificed Himself so that men might be free from physical imperfections...to give the spirit of life...

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:9, p. 156 (*)
lippitt.note: All the Prophets appeared that the oneness of man might be taught.  How much suffering these Prophets had to endure to unfold this illumination.  His Holiness offered His life. (*Talk in Stuttgart, 4/6/1913)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:18, p. 179 (*)
lippitt.note: Let us have the life of the spirit...of sympathy, practical cooperation, celestial brotherhood... (*Talk, 5/1914)
It is for this purpose that the Manifestations unveil themselves, bear every ordeal to make ready soul's dawning of light.  This is the real atonement of Christ.  He sacrificed Himself for the world.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 632
lippitt.note: The Christ sacrificed Himself so that men might be free from the imperfections of the physical nature...
Jesus manifested Himself in Jerusalem and called men to the Kingdom of God.
Literal interpretation veiled Jesus from the Jews, even though they had been waiting for the Messiah.
God, however, aided Him and protected Him.  The Lord preserveth whom He willeth.
The enemies of His Holiness Christ were the Jews, the Pharisees but the enemies of Baha'u'llah were kings representing 15,000,000 people.  At last His banner was upraised...

lippitt.ref: Utterances in letter of Mrs. Hoagg to Mrs. (*)
lippitt.note: In the time of Jesus the Jewish clergy created so many doubts that there were very few who believed--in reality there were only twelve and one of those denied Him.  Why? Because tests were very great at that time. (*Brittingham, 1/1914)
Illustration of sedition and Covenant-breaking.  Covenant-breakers are execrated and condemned.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 110
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah came in the station of the Father, fulfilling this prophecy.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIII:1, p. 21
lippitt.note: In the holy Writings of His Holiness Baha'u'llah in a thousand places at least, the violators of the Covenant are execrated and condemned.
Baha'u'llah came in the station and glory of the Father and the Lord of the vineyard.
Warning to evil doers about Baha'u'llah.
Baha'u'llah identifies Himself with the Promised Father...
This is the experience of the Manifestations.
Beware that thou dispute not with Baha'u'llah even as the Pharisees disputed with Jesus.  He Who is the Lord of Lords is come overshadowed with clouds.  He verily hath come down again.
Certain persons, like the Pharisees...will exert themselves to deprive souls of the glad-tidings of the Holy Spirit.
The Jews expected the Messiah but when He came they rejected Him--they were bereft of sight...Their eyes were covered with veils of traditions and names.
Baha'u'llah warns the Pope not to dispute with Him as did the Pharisees with Jesus.
The Jews rejected Jesus; they were blind to the Reality of Christ.  Literal interpretation of the prophecies veiled them.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VI:6, p. 43
lippitt.note: They held strictly to the law, there were the causes of the condemnation and crucifixion of Jesus.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 633 (*Bahai)
lippitt.note: The Jews cried, O God, hasten the day when the Promised One will manifest Himself.  He claims to be from Nazareth. He was to come with a sword; sit on the throne of David; conquer the East and West;teach the law of Moses...(*Scriptures,p.729-30)

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 73
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah explained that to obey rulers is to obey God.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 81, 89
lippitt.note: To obey rulers is to obey God.
Live so that the world may see you loyal to God and to your just government.  This is justice.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 7 (*)
lippitt.note: Religious interests should not be brought into politics.  Reliigions should treat of morals.  Politics of material circumstances. (*Addresses in Paris and London)

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 41
lippitt.note: Comparison betwee the response to God's Call, and rejection by the divines of Baha'u'llah.
The Call of Baha'u'llah surpassed that of Moses from the Burning Bush.

Under such appalling circumstances the Most Great Spirit, symbolized in the Zoroastrian, the Mosaic, the Christian and Muhammadan Dispensations by the Sacred Fire, the Burning Bush, the Dove, and the angel Gabriel...
The experience of Moses in receiving the Call of God from the Burning Bush symbolic of the Annunciation of the angel to Baha'u'llah regarding His Mission.
This is the original and principle teaching that Jesus Christ spread in the world.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:17, p. 171
lippitt.note: All the Prophets were sent, all the books were revealed, so that the law of love might be promoted.
We see in these days the fulfilment of divine prophecy.  Jerusalem has disappeared...now it is rebuilt.
Every time He made reply, He set forth a special sign that should herald the promised Dispensation.
men will lament the loss of the Sun of divine beauty, the Moon of knowledge, the stars of divine wisdom.  Thereupon they will behold the countenance of the Promised One...
The revelation of Jesus to His disciples analogous to the statement "commotions shall appear and the voice of lamentation shall be raised and evidences of mischief shall be revealed."

lippitt.ref: Wisdom of 'Abdu'l-Baha, p. 44; Paris Talks, p. 49
lippitt.note: Apostles of Christ steadfastly bore their trials and sufferings with endurance and faithfulness.  Those who suffer most attain greatest perfection.

lippitt.ref: Flowers...from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 5
lippitt.note: Jesus referred to the time when the abomination of desolation shall take place.
Prophesied by Daniel and confirmed by Jesus Christ accomplished by the coming of the Bab.
This ended with the coming of the Bab.

The Apostles when they followed Jesus Christ...their ignorance gave place to understanding...they became spiritual...they became sons of God...they became saints.  Pray to God that you may become as angels and beacons of light.

browser.central_figures:
Gems of Divine Mysteries, p. 8, paragraph 9
link

lippitt.note: By the sun in one sense is meant the Suns of Truth Who fill the world with a liberal effusion of grace from on high...

Hence it is manifest that by the words "the sun shall be darkened and the moon shall not give her light" is intended the waywardness of the divines and the annulment of laws established by divine Revelation...
Explanation of the sun, moon, stars and the tribulation.
Christians are expecting the stars to fall and Christ to appear in the clouds, yet these are but symbols.  Jews awaiting Christ from a heaven which does not exist.
His coming depends upon the fulfilment of certain signs...

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 27
lippitt.note: Science proves that all the stars are much larger than the earth.  The Kingdom of heaven is within your soul.
The stars of heaven of knowledge have fallen--they adduced the proofs in order to demonstrate the truth of Baha'u'llah's Cause.
Baha'u'llah proclaims the fall of the stars of the firmament who have turned aside from Him.
He Who is the Lord of Lords is come overshadowed with clouds and the decree hath been fulfilled.
Explanation of the meanings of clouds.

Were the prophecies recorded in the Gospel to be literally fulfilled, were Jesus to descend from heaven upon the clouds, who would dare to disbelieve.  No soul would feel about to accept or reject the truth.
To Him Jesus Christ had referred as the Son of man who shall come in the glory of His Father, in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
As the clouds hide the sun so the humanity of Christ hid His divine character.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VIII:13, p. 180 (*)
lippitt.note: Explanation of symbolism of clouds, sun, etc. (*Tablet for Mirza Abu'l-Fadl and 'Ali Quli Khan for 100th anniversary of the birth of Baha'u'llah)
The cloud mentioned in the Holy Scriptures is the human body...a veil preventing them from seeing Christ.
There is a difference among believers as to the Second Coming of Christ.  The Promised Christ is Baha'u'llah and the Bab.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 45
lippitt.note: Some are awaiting the coming of Christ in the clouds.  He has already come in the heart if you believe...
They see the body but not the Spirit...they hear the word but are unable to understand the meaning...Clouds are obstacles, the human body was the obstacle that prevented Jews from seeing the Christ.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 27
lippitt.note: Failure of the kings of Christendom to draw near unto Baha'u'llah.
Who shall come with all His holy angels about Him and all nations gathered before His throne--Baha'u'llah fulfilled these conditions.
I hope you may be quickened and vivified by the breaths of the Holy Spirit.  Then shall ye become the angels of heaven whom Christ promised would appear to gather the harvest of divine planting.
The meaning, there are two generations, the one spiritual and the other physical.  Christ was speaking of the spiritual generation.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 143
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah declares this is the great and terrible Day foretold in the Gospel.

The second coming of Christ will be in like manner--the signs spoken of are not to be taken literally.  Christ said, "Perhaps I shall come when you are yet asleep, for the coming of the Son of man is like the coming of a thief."
Thou wast awake, therefore thou didst advance and engage in the service of the Word of God.

browser.central_figures:
Gems of Divine Mysteries, p. 17, paragraph 22
link

lippitt.note: Jews were awaiting the Messiah...when He came, Jews rejected Jesus.  The same is the case with Baha'u'llah.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 48-49
lippitt.note: When I came to them in My glory, they turned aside...The Jewish doctors disputed with Jesus and perpetrated what hath made the Holy Spirit lament.
In embalming they washed and perfumed the dead.  Christ was to be crucified so she annointed Him and dried it with her hair.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VIII:11, p. 144
lippitt.note: The perfumed oil bottle represents the heart of Mary; the ointment is the love of God in Mary's heart.  The poor in this sense meant those who needed love.  We must always cherish the poor for His sake.

lippitt.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 42
lippitt.note: The ancient embalming they washed and perfumed the dead.  Christ was to be crucified and so she annointed Him and dried it with her hair.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XII:9, p. 157 (*)
lippitt.note: There was one name that always brought joy to the face of Baha'u'llah.  His expression would change at the metnion of it...it was Mary Magdalene. (*Juliet Thompson's Diary?)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIV:12, p. 353 (*)
lippitt.note: Were it not for the tests the face of Mary, the Magdalene, would not glisten with the light of firmness and certainty unto all horizons. (*Tablet to Mrs. Brittingham?)
Be pure--to be pure is to be selfless.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:14, p. 270 (*)
lippitt.note: Judas Iscariot first believed in Christ then turned away from Him.  I hope that thou mayest daily increase in firmness and become the cause of the guidance of other souls.  (*Tablet to American believer)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 242  (*)
lippitt.note: The Covenant is an ocean; violators are the foam.  They shall be completely forgotten, but the ocean of the Covenant is eternally surging.  They will be forgotten like Judas and his associates. (*Tablet to Mr. & Mrs. MacNutt, Brooklyn)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIII:1, p. 21,25
lippitt.note: Judas separated himself from Jesus.  Any person who has left the Cause did so because of his own actions, intrigues and evil plots.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:14, p. 269 (*)
lippitt.note: Violators like Judas Iscariot have for financial motives deviated from the Covenant.  Baha'i unity cannot be preserved save through the Covenant of God. (*Tablet to Miss Mary Lesch)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IX:2, p. 23(Talk in Haifa 12/22)
lippitt.note: When Jesus was crucified He had twelve disciples...one denied Him...another sold Him for a little money.  Notwithstanding this, consider how important was His Cause.  There were at least 100,000 souls who would have sacrificed their lives for Baha
because there was excessive love among them...that table surpassed the royal tables...

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:16, p. 269
lippitt.note: It is well to worship in any place...the object is to turn to God.  When Christ gathered His disciples together for the Last Supper, it was not in a church, it was in a room.  The heart should be attracted, spirit filled with Divine Glad Tidings

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:16, p. 275 (*)
lippitt.note: The manna which came from heaven for the disciples was bounty, knowledge, faith, assurance, love and attraction, attachment and enkindlement...My hope is that that gathering may also eat from this manna and bounties.  (*Tablet to Mr. Hall)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVIII:9, p. 284
lippitt.note: Because there was excessive love among the individual members who had convened there, that table surpassed the royal tables and it was established as the Lord's Supper--even now it is known as such.
Make an effort in every meeting that the Lord's Supper may become realized and heavenly food--love, affinity, union of souls, descend...then 'Abdu'l-Baha will also be present.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:12, p. 203 (*)
lippitt.note: Inasmuch as there was present material bread and likewise heavenly manna which was descending upon them; it was verily the Lord's Supper. (*Talk at 19 day feast, 10/16/1912, home of Mr. Helen Goodall)
If the foot of man deviate as did that of Judas, so that he became human after he had been divine...there is in that a great lesson to people of understanding.
Designated as the Lord's Supper, they ate heavenly food at that supper--the love, knowledge, mysteries and bestowal of God.
Symbols signifying bounties and perfections...
You have written about the nineteen day feast.  This arrangement of affection causeth everlasting life.
The Supper was a spiritual meal.  This heavenly food consisted in the love, knowledge, mysteries and bestowal of God.  Religious leaders thought material food was changed into spiritual food.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 74
lippitt.note: This is the blessed Supper of the Lord for we have gathered under the shadow of the Blessed Perfection...This was indeed a spiritual feast where Love reigned.

lippitt.ref: Flowers...from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 27 (*)
lippitt.note: Sacrifice of Jesus proved His love for mankind, followers believed in Him and were delivered from sin through their obediance and changed lives. (*Talk by Mirza Haydar 'Ali, 11/11/1908, Akka)
These verses have been interpreted by the churches in such a superstitious way that it is impossible for human reason to accept the explanation.
I gave My blood for the life of the world...Those who turned towards Christ were saved from their attachment to sin.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XXII:9, p. 258 (*)
lippitt.note: He came people say to redeem us through His blood.  He sacrificed His life that He might educate humanity and illumine the world of reality. (*Tablet)

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 90
lippitt.note: It is evident that Christ did not mean material grapes, but it was a spiritual state which He interpreted as fruit.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, II:15, p. 16 (*)
lippitt.note: It is evident His Holiness Christ did not mean material grapes but it was a spiritual condition and a heavenly state which He interpreted as fruit. (*Tablet)
The appearance of the Holy Manifestations is for the purpose of causing the heavenly table to descend.  This table means divine virtues and characteristics.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 69
lippitt.note: Someday the Rizwan will be visited by pilgrims just as the Garden of Gethsemane is sacred with memories of Jesus Christ.
Whatever troubles we have on our way to the Kingdom are a test to the soul.  The way to God is strewn with troubles and difficulties.  Remember what Christ said, the spirit is powerful.
The spirit of man is powerful but the body is weak and incapable.  It is intended that the spirit triumphs.
Judas chose to turn from Jesus and go his own way.  'Abdu'l-Baha was rejected by the violators.

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 98
lippitt.note: Verily, tests withheld a great Apostle from the mercy of God and made him take part in the shedding of the blood of Jesus Christ.
The betrayal of Baha'u'llah more shameful than the betrayal of Jesus Christ.
Baha'u'llah has left no room for dissension--some wish to create this as Judas did.  Violators are not to be accepted.
Impossible to train another until he first conquers his own self and his desires...Judas was deprived of the Kingdom of God.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
lippitt.note: Judas, on of the twelve disciples of Jesus Christ betrayed Him.  God will assist all who are firm in His Cause.
There were people like Judas, who, by their own deeds, separated themselves from Christ [similar to violators who turned against 'Abdu'l-Baha].

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 3 (*)
lippitt.note: Judas began well but failed in the end.  Paul was an enemy of Christ but he became one of his best servants.  It is wrong to judge--the end of man is not known.  (* From discourses in London and Paris)
Judas turned away on account of jealousy of Peter.  Consider the predecessors as a warning and purify thy heart...
Christ taught, Resist not evil.  When we hold fast to the foundation of religion, differences will disappear.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:3, p. 55 (*)
lippitt.note: When we hold fast to the foundations of the religions of God all differences are dispelled. (* Answers to questions asked by theologians in Paris)

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 48-49
lippitt.note: When I came to them in My glory, they turned aside...The Jewish doctors disputed with Jesus and perpetrated what hath made the Holy Spirit lament.
Scribes and Pharisees spread slanders and caused Him to be crucified.
Though Jesus threatened to destroy the Temple it did not hinder the spread of the Gospel.
Observe how that persecution and scorn were changed to glory.  The Jews scoffed...saying, This man is naught but a sorcerer, blasphemeth and is possessed of a devil.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 27
lippitt.note: Failture of the kings of Christendom to draw near unto Baha'u'llah.
Peter who was the Rock [of the Christian Dispensation] was firm and solid...but the Rock quaked a little.  I hope you will become steadfast, firm, solid...

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons Received at Acca, p. 98
lippitt.note: The tests made Peter the Apostle deny Christ.  Many a just soul did not endure the power of tests.
At that time Peter was weak for he had not yet received the baptism of the Holy Spirit' afterwards he became firm to the end.

Christ only educated eleven men; the greatest of them was Peter who, when tested, thrice denied Christ.  In spite of this the Cause of Christ permeated the world.  Reflect what will take place in the future.  Baha'u'llah educated thousands.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IX:2, p. 23(Talk in Haifa 12/22)
lippitt.note: When Jesus Christ was crucified He had twelve disciples...one denied Him...another sold Him for a little money.  Notwithstanding this, consider how important was His Cause.  There were at least 100,000 souls who would sacrifice their lives for Baha

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:12, p. 207 (*)
lippitt.note: Comparison between men and women: Peter and Mary Magdalene. (* Interview with San Francisco newspaper reporter)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIV:12, p. 353 (*)
lippitt.note: Even the glorious Peter was not rescued from the flame of trials...and wavered.  (*Extract from Tablet)
This was before the Holy Spirit had descended on Him at Pentecost.
Comparison between the conduct of Peter and that of Mary Magdalene at the time of the crucifixion of Jesus Christ...a proof that woman is equivalent and complement of man.
The great Peter denied Jesus and the Jews scoffed at Him and despised Him, yet the Fire they thought they had quenched kindled the hearts of the righteous.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 33
lippitt.note: Peter was the greatest of all the disciples yet he denied Jesus Christ three times.  What happened afterwards?  The Truth in Christ spread all over the world.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 31
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah warned the Pope, Guard thyself lest darkness spread its veils over thee.
Adversaries of Jesus were rabbis...whereas those of the Bab represented the civil and ecclesiastical powers who conspired against the upholders and the Revelation.
Treatment of Baha'u'llah analogous to that of Jesus Christ at the hands of ecclesiastical leaders.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:1, p. 18
lippitt.note: Christians point out that this was a wonderful event, that His Holiness without opposition from the Jews was permitted to enter their most sacred place and proclaim the Word of God...

The Pharisees had evil opinions of Jesus Christ.  But the radiance of His illumination was diffused over the whole world; they regarded even the ass on which He was mounted as a throne, and the thorns a brilliant diadem.
Consider how they crowned Jesus with a crown of thorns...now how thousands of people adorn their heads in His name with the most brilliant gems.  Woe! woe unto such heedless people.
His abasement was His glorification; His crown of thorns is a heavenly diadem.  When they pressed it upon His blessed head...they laid the foundation of His everlasting Kingdom...

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 100
lippitt.note: Sufferings of Jesus analogous to those of Prophets caused by divines.  Baha'u'llah recalls the sufferings of the Prophets.
The Pharisees persecuted Jesus Christ, the result was that His lamp became ignited, His light began to shine...
Of the same class are the Pharisees and priests in this manifest day, they are isolated from the gifts of your Lord...
Alas for the souls because of what they committed against the Word of God.
The Jews did not know the meaning of the prophecies.  Literal interpretation veiled the true meaning.
There was no abuse and persecution which they did not heap upon Him.
They did not investigate the truth.  This is one of the Baha'i principles.
Jews rose up against Jesus but He is now glorified.  They did not succeed in their efforts to stop the establishment of the Kingdom.  God's dominion is eternal and will be set up.
All the prophets of God were persecuted.  Jesus Christ suffered martyrdom in order to summon mankind to unity and love.  It is the divine purpose of religion.
The Jews rejected Jesus and tortured and crucified Him.  All Prophets were scorned and persecuted.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:9, p. 156 (*)
lippitt.note: He endured all things so that the world might unite and that He might cement the hearts of men through His love. (*Address in Stuttgart, Germany on 4/6/1913)
Baha'u'llah recalled in anticipation of His own sufferings those of Jesus Christ.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 13
lippitt.note: The world must come to know the Word in Christ.  His mission was to uplift the very world which refused Him.
All through His life He suffered oppression and hardship.  Humanity was His enemy.  In spite of all this He was the embodiment of pity, supreme goodness and love.
Jesus Christ knew that all earthly diadems were at His feet, therefore He said, All power is given Me in heaven and in earth.
To all outward appearances they overcame Him but all the kings of the earth have not been able to withstand Him.
Jesus Christ, the Bab and Baha'u'lah suffered for mankind because of their love.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IX:15, p. 171 (*)
lippitt.note: Later on it became manifest that He was the light of Truth and was betowing the spirit of eternal life--No Prophet cometh unto them but they laugh Him to scorn. (*Tablet for Juanita Storch)

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 19
lippitt.note: The enemies of Christ persecuted Him and crucified Him yet He loved them all.  If the love of God is shining in our hearts we will love all alike.
No death of religion-founder comparable to that of the Bab except in the Gospels.
The Talmud and its superstitions veiled His Holiness so completely that they crucified their promised Messiah.
Blind imitations and hereditary prejudices have become the cause of hatred...therefore we must seek truth.
The Jews crucified Jesus.  Because Jews were captives to ancestral imitations they denied His Holiness Jesus.  We must forsake all such beliefs.
If the Jews had investigated the reality of Christ they would have beheld His beauty and truth.  They were withheld because of bigoted imitations.
Before the appearance in human form, the Word of God was in the utmost sanctity and glory...when it shone in the body it fell into the hands of the Jews and at last was crucified.
He rose to teach and educate men and so He sacrificed Himself to give the spirit of life.  He perished in body so as to quicken others by His spirit.

In a revealed prayer Baha'u'llah exalts Mirza Mihdi's death to the rank of those acts of atonement associated with Abraham's intended sacrifice, the crucifixion of Jesus Christ and the martyrdom of the Imam Husayn.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 100
lippitt.note: Suffering inflicted upon Jesus Christ by the priests similar to that inflicted on other martyrs by divines.
Jesus gave His life that this world might be released from discord and strife, for the unity of mankind.
may you offer yourselves in the threshold of sacrifice for the betterment of the whole world.
They sentenced Him to death.  Why did this happen?  Because they were blindly following imitations, the Jews rejected Jesus.  They refused to investigate Truth.
knowing what would happen, He arose to proclaim His Message...offered His life as a sacrifice for humanity.

lippitt.ref: Utterances in letter from Emogene Hoagg to Mrs(*)
lippitt.note: The Jewish clergy were busy creating doubts in the minds of the people...Therefore they say Jesus was a false Messiah.  Those who have weak faith will surely be shaken.(* I.D.Brittingham, Haifa, 1/1914, translated by Monever Khanum)
The crucifixion was the means of spreading the light of the Cause of God.
In the time of Jesus cruelty and tyranny were such that people crucified even Jesus.  Literal interpretation veiled the Truth from the Jews.  They had no knowledge of the symbology of the Scriptures.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:1, p. 14 (Lawh-i-Aqdas)
lippitt.note: Remember, the most learned doctors of His country in His age condemned Him to be killed whilst one who was the catcher of fishes believed in Him.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:2, p. 40 (*)
lippitt.note: He sacrificed His life that the oil in the lamps of our souls might become ignited and His light shine from them. (*Talk in New York City, 6/11/1912)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, V:11, p. 169
lippitt.note: they crucified their promised Messiah...

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XV:9, p. 254 (*)
lippitt.note: Jesus Christ gave His life on the cross for the unity of mankind.  Only a few were attracted to Him but with this small army He conquered the world. (*Talk in New York City)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 633 (*)
lippitt.note: Some Jews cried, O God, hasten the Day when the Promised One will manifest Himself.  When He came they knew Him not...they killed Him...they could not see the Reality of Christ. (*Bahai Scriptures, p. 729-30)
Christ suffered.  He accepted all His trials because of the immortality of His spirit.
Christ suffered unto death to bring Light...All the prophets of God came to promote unity.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 31
lippitt.note: All Manifestations have been rejected and persecuted; men have worshipped personality but were blind to their Reality and Truth.
The body of Jesus was crucified but the Spirit of Christ is always pouring upon the world...

And at length He gave His life in sacrifice for humanity...His heart was full of love for all the world, His goodness was destined to reach each one--as they began to realize these things they repented...
The Jews were blind to Reality.  Literal interpretation prevented their seeing the Beauty of Christ, and they crucified Him.
The Jews rose against Jesus Christ even to the shedding of His blood.  Had they investigated Reality they would have accepted their promised Messiah, Jesus Christ.
Treatment of Baha'u'llah by divines analogous to that of Jesus Christ by the priests.

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 62
lippitt.note: Because it is clear literal text it is believed.  Explanation of symbolism.
As Christ came from a spiritual heaven of the Divine Kingdom, therefore His disappearance under the earth for three days has an inner signification and is not an outward fact.
Mary Magdalene kept firm in the Cause.  She surpassed men in defending the Cause.  This is a manifest Light.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:12, p. 232 (*)
lippitt.note: Mary Magdalene, only a peasant, gained a precedence of the queens of the world who have left no trace, but Mary Magdalene is like unto a star. (*Tablet to Mrs. Annie L. Parmerton, Cincinnati, Ohio)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:17, p. 307 (*)
lippitt.note: Later on it became evident what crowns studded with brilliant gems of guidance were placed on the heads of Mary Magdalene and the other Mary, the Mother of Jesus. (*Unveiling of Divine Plan for Islands of the Sea, read 1919 at 11th Convention)

Christ had twelve disciples, Judas became a traitor, the remaining disciples were wavering.  It is certain that the one who re-established their faith was Mary Magdalene.  The world consists of two factors--male and female.  Each is the complement
Comparison between Peter and Mary Magdalene, an example of the capability of woman.  Baha'u'llah emancipated women.
Mary Magdalene was transformed and became the means through which the confirmation of God descended upon the disciples.
Trust in the bounty and grace of God and rest assured in the bestowals of His eternal outpouring.
Mary Magdalene strengthened the disciples.  In the sight of God sex makes no difference.  He or she is greatest who is nearest to God.

lippitt.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose garden at Acca, p.15
lippitt.note: After she had believed in Christ observe the transformation, see her glory.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
lippitt.note: Mary Magdalene held fastly to the Christ and made others firm.  God will assist all who are firm in His Cause.
Mary Magdalene was superior to all the men of her time.  She gathered the others together and said, "Why do ye mourn?"  Her reality is shining from the horizon of Christ.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:10, p. 85 (*)
lippitt.note: Through this superhuman service the rays of peace and conciliation may illumine and enlighten all the regions and the world of humanity may find peace. (*Tablet to the West)

lippitt.note: After Him came His chosen Apostles and they went far and wide carrying the light of their Master's teaching into a dark world.
Consider the influence of Jesus Christ on His Apostles.  These simple men were enabled by the Power of the Holy Spirit to spread the glad tidings.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:2, p. 41 (*)
lippitt.note: We must be willing to give our lives.  We must go among the people of the world, summoning them. (*Talk at 309 West 78th Street, New York City, 1912)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, V:14, p. 215 (*)
lippitt.note: We must act accordingly and follow in their footsteps. (*Trumpet call to the East and West)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, V:1, p. 6
lippitt.note: Command to travel in every country...as did the Apostles of Jesus Christ.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:14, p. 131
lippitt.note: Consider with what severance, attraction and enkindlement the apostles arose in the promulgation of the word of God.

lippitt.ref: Utterances in Emogene Hoagg to Mrs. I.D. (*)
lippitt.note: The Jewish clergy said according to prophecy he should come from heaven in the promised Day and this John the Baptist had a father and a mother.  The clergy were busy creating doubts in the minds of the people.
It was Yahya son of Zachariah who gave unto the people the tidings of the Manifestation of Jesus.
John the Baptist, fore-runner or herald of Jesus Christ.  Virtues of John the Baptist similar to those of Elias.
Christ came from heaven, though outwardly He came from the womb of Mary.
Were a myriad voices be raised, no ear would listen if upon a fatherless child hath been conferred Prophethood.
Physically Jesus was born of a woman but the Christ Reality came from the city of Eternity.
Jesus came from heaven though outwardly He came from the womb of Mary.
The Reality, perfections and Spirit of Christ came from God but He was born of Mary.
Though Jesus said He came from heaven, they knew that He was born of Mary.
Jesus' Reality from heaven, his body earthly.  Literal interpretation veiled the truth from the Jews.
Were a myriad voices to be raised, no ear would listen if upon a fatherless child hath been conferred Prophethood.
If Muhammadans and Christians would investigate the truth the outcome would be unity.
Jesus came to the world through the power of God, born of the Holy Spirit and of the blessed Virgin Mary.  Mary His mother was a saint from heaven...God made Mary to be exalted above all other women.
The people of Islam were taught to realize how Jesus came from God and was born of the Spirit and that He must be glorified of all men.
How was Christ born of the Holy Ghost?  (Answered)
The Christ is born of the Holy Spirit; He is raised up by the Holy Spirit; He is a descendant of the Holy Spirit; the Reality of Christ does not descend from Adam.  Christ is a quickening Spirit.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VIII:8, p. 98
lippitt.note: Christ was born of the Holy Spirit.  He was the sun and His rays shone upon His disciples through His teachings...

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVII:10, p. 328
lippitt.note: In the Qur'an it states that Jesus Christ came into the world through the quickening breaths of the Holy Spirit...O Arabians, you must believe in Jesus Christ as a product of the Holy Spirit.
In reality Jesus did descend from heaven although He was born of Mary.  He was a heavenly man.
In the Kitab-i-Iqan Baha'u'llah upholds the purity and innocence of the Virgin Mary.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 113
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah designated Jesus as One who appeared out of the breath of the Holy Ghost.
Abdul-Baha saw in a radiant vision the world of the future when the divine Message having become comprehended by men of good will would change the heart of the world.
Though Jesus said He came from heaven, many knew that He was born of Mary.
Thou born of Mary, the Christ was the divine spirit sent down from heaven.  So will the Second Coming be.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XV:5, p. 135 (Tablet)
lippitt.note: Although He was of such poor and humble condition that He was born in a manger, yet He changed the whole world by His power and divinity.  How can anyone deny His proof?  Baha'u'llah did not go to school yet much knowledge was manifest in Him...

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XXIII:9, p. 266
lippitt.note: Christ's birthday is a glorious day.  The advent of Christ was a blessed day, for it was the day on which the Sun of Reality dawned; the day on which all beings were revivified.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 32
lippitt.note: Jesus Christ was so poor that he was born in a manger yet He changed the conditions of the world--proof of His manifestationship.
Prophecy of world peace.  Abdu'l-Baha saw a radiant vision the world of the future when the Divine Message having become comprehended by men of good will would change the heart of the world...
'Abdu'l-Baha pleaded with the Muhammadans to investigate the truth concerning Jesus in the Qur'an and the Bible.  If Muhammadans and Christians would investigate truth the outcome would be unity.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 45
lippitt.note: Elijah came but was not recognized in John...not the entity of Elijah but his qualities that John embodied...
Yahya is John the Baptist, the Wrod is Jesus and the message is: Repent for the Kingdom of Heaven is at hand.
Baptism...

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 36
lippitt.note: Each Manifestation came after a herald.  Ahmad and Kazim were forerunners, before the Manifestations of this Day.
Return of the personality or of individuality is denied but return of perfections in Elijah and John the Baptist.
Behold how this wronged One is now surrounded by a generation of vipers.  He is summoning all men to Him, warning the heedless.
This spirit of faith is the flame of reality, the life of humanity and the cause of eternal illumination.
Baptism and fire are one.  By the fire of the love of God the veil is burnt which separates us from Heavenly realities.
He who is baptized by the Holy Spirit is a manifestation of divine mercy to mankind.
Christ appeared in order to illumine the world of humanity...to enkindle the light of love in human hearts.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IX:9, p. 103
lippitt.note: Water symbolizes the knowledge of God which gives eternal life because all life had its beginning in water.  John baptized with water, symbol of purity...These are ceremonies which have spiritual meaning but no spiritual effect upon the soul...
Most Great Spirit designated by Baha'u'llah symbolized in Christian Dispensation as the Dove.
The call of Baha'u'llah surpassed the annunciation to Jesus Christ at His Baptism.
The descent of a dove upon Jesus Christ symbolic of the Annunciation of the angel to Baha'u'llah regarding His mission.
Christ desired this institution to be used at this time so He Himself conformed to it.
Two-fold nature of Manifestations.  Before His baptism the Holy Spirit did not manifest itself in Him.  Explanation of human, and as One with God.
God conferred upon that Essence of the Spirit, Who was known amongst the people as fatherless, the glory of Prophethood, and made Him His testimony unto all that are in heaven and on earth.
Baha'u'llah declared the divines did not know the station of Jesus Christ.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 113
lippitt.note: The sonship of Jesus Christ asserted by Baha'u'llah.
This is not a material dove...it was a spiritual state expressed by a figure.  Symbolism abounds in the Scriptures.
The sonship station is the heart of Christ, and the Holy Spirit is the station of the Spirit of Christ.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIV:9, p. 274
lippitt.note: The story about the dove is simply a metaphor.  No dove came down.  The Holy Spirit was always with Christ.  He knew about His mission from early childhood.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 45
lippitt.note: Manifestations are expected but when the come, they are rejected because of the blindness of people.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:18, p. 305
lippitt.note: In the beginning the disciples and Christians fasted.  Later they changed fasting into lenten observances...
The body of Christ was born of Mary of Nazareth but the Spirit was of God...the strength of His Spirit was immeasurable.
The Jews, knowing He lived in Nazareth would not accept His statement that He came down from Heaven.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:1, p. 14 (*)
lippitt.note: Open your minds, the Spirit standeth behind them. (*Baha'u'llah, Lawh-i-Aqdas)
Christ was an educator.  He taught mankind by the power of the Holy Spirit.  His teachings were altruistic; His bestowal universal.
His Holiness Jesus Christ was a real physician.  Prophets of God are real physicians--the prescribe for human conditions...whoever is healed by that remedy finds eternal health...

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 103
lippitt.note: The effect of a manifestation is to drive out all that is evil and replace the natural growth of virtues...

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 31
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah called the attention of the Pope to the analogy of simple fishermen and rejection of the learned.
Through the aid and assistance of God they became mighty ones of God.  Peter was transformed.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 6-7 (*)
lippitt.note: Peter had no education.  He was transformed by the power of the Holy Spirit.  He who is educated by the Holy Spirit can in turn educate others. (*From discourses in Paris and London)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:15, p. 258 (*)
lippitt.note: The Apostles were fishers.  Consider to what a high station they did rise.  Their sovereignty runs to eternity (*Tablet to Roy C. Williams [Wilhelm?])
Through the power of the Holy Spirit these simple fishermen were able to do what philosophers and scientists failed to accomplish.

lippitt.ref: The Advent of Divine Justice, p. 38
lippitt.note: Peter was the first Apostle to be called.  Observe how this crude and helpless instrument was transformed by the power of the Christ.  How much greater the power of the Father in Baha'u'llah.
Baha'u'llah gives a greater promise to His followers than that of Jesus to Peter.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 110
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah reminded the Christians of the response to Jesus Christ's call--called them to become the quickeners of mankind.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 110
lippitt.note: Jesus said, Come after Me and I will make you to become fishers of men.  In this day We say, Come ye after Me that We may make you to become quickeners of mankind.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 124
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah asserts...fishers of men, individual; quickeners of mankind, universal.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IX:10, p. 112 (*Talk on 6/13/14)
lippitt.note: Many common souls and the most degraded advanced and followed the divine Manifestations.  Everyone testifies that the learned philosophers and nobles of every nation were humble in the presence of the Blessed Beauty, and great numbers of these becami

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:3, p. 39 (*)
lippitt.note: Do not look at present condition, contemplate the future.  Insgnificant persons--how great they became!  Bounties of Baha'u'llah shall in the future be made manifest.  (*Tablet to Mrs. Agnes Parsons,1/8/1919, translated by Shoghi Effendi)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIII:7, p. 173 (DMAS)
lippitt.note: Unity and concord is their bait: service and self-sacrifice is their sport.  Let the Baha'is be the fishermen of the sea of spirituality.  Let them not lose their courage...

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:12, p. 232 (*)
lippitt.note: After the baptism of the Holy Spirit Peter became a great preacher. (*Tablet to American Baha'i)
Jesus Christ renewed the Commandments.
Jesus spread the name of Moses throughout the Orient and Occident.  We must investigate reality--banish heresay from our minds.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 633 (*)
lippitt.note: When Jesus Christ appeared amongst the Jews, the first thing He did was to proclaim the validity of the Mosaic Mission.  He declared that the Torah, the Old Testament was the Word of God... (*Bahai Scriptures, p. 729)
If you give the same teachings to all some might be retarded by them instead of helped.
This is the real sovereignty and such is God's power in His Chosen Ones.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 48
lippitt.note: Analogy between the doctors disputing and rejecting Jesus and the divines rejecting Baha'u'llah.

The greatest friend of Moses was Jesus but the Jews felt He was taking away some of Moses' glory.  They were veiled from the truth and kept away from His bounties.

lippitt.note: Manifestations changed some of the laws of the Jews, which they called the Law of Moses...Jesus changed laws regarding the Sabbath because of the needs and conditions of the times...therefore they turned away from Him.

lippitt.ref: Utterances in letter from H.E. Hoagg to Mrs. (*)
lippitt.note: The Jewish clergy said, The Promised One must promote the laws of the Bible and this man broke the Sabbath.  The clergy created doubts in the minds of the people. (* I.D. Brittingham, Haifa 1/1914, translated by Monever Khanum)
The Jews said the Messiah was to spread the law of Moses, but Jesus seeks to destroy it.  They were blind to Reality.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 239 (*Tablet to)
lippitt.note: They declared that Christ was a breaker of the Sabbath. Although on the surface they seemed to defend the Cause of God, yet inwardly their purpose was manifest error.  Violators...show insincere love. (*Mr. & Mrs. Howard MacNutt)
The Jews believed the Messiah would promulgate all the laws of Moses; when Jesus abrogated the law of the Sabbath the people rose against Him.
Manifestations change the material laws in conformity with the needs and conditions of the times.  Jews were veiled from reality by ancestral traditions.
Jesus changed laws in regard to the Sabbath.  This was according to the spirit of the age, but as these laws were not expedient in the time of Christ, they were abrogated.
He reformed the law of Moses, therefore the Jews rejected Him.
Though Jesus Christ abrogated the law of Moses concerning the Sabbath, it did not prevent the spread of the Gospel.
Jesus Christ gave the spiritual truth regarding the Sabbath...the Jews were veiled from the truth by narrow literal interpretations.
In the days of Jesus, conditions had changed, Mosaic Laws not adapted to the needs and conditions, therefore He abrogated the Law concerning the Sabbath.

lippitt.ref: Visit at Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 31
lippitt.note: The Sabbath of the Jews was changed by Christ.  The outward rules may be changed but the reality is unchanging.
Jews were veiled from truth by literal interpretations; therefore they rejected Jesus Christ.
Because Jesus Christ did not observe the Sabbath according to the Scriptures, they rejected Him.  Literal interpretation and narrow practices led them to reject Jesus Christ.

His Holiness Christ proclaimed and completed the Law of Moses.  He abolished certain laws which were not in accord with needs and conditions of the times.  Jews rejected Jesus because of adherence to ancient forms.
Only eleven became apostles of Jesus Christ; they illumined the world.  Similar conditions in Paris...little by little the Cause grew.
Baha'u'llah has revoiced and re-established the quintescence of the teachings of all the Prophets.
The Jews did not understand, therefore they were debarred from perceiving the truth of Christ.
Jesus Christ really had only eleven disciples as Judas was evil and betrayed Him.
Consider the days of Christ, how the light of guidance brightened twelve hearts.  The lamp of guidance has been lighted in your hearts.
The disciples were watching and observing their Master's conduct, followed His example and after His ascension scattered to various regions.  In the same way we must follow Baha'u'llah.
The product of the life of Christ was the illumined souls of those who believed in Him.

Christ in reality educated only eleven men, of whom one thrice denied Him.  The Cause of Christ permeated the world.  Baha'u'llah has educated thousands of souls.  Then reflect what will take place in the future.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 3 (*)
lippitt.note: Judas began well but failed in the end.  Paul was an enemy of Christ but he became one of His best servants.  It is wrong to judge, the end of man is not known. (*Discourse)

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
lippitt.note: Twelve believed in Him; one deserted Him, others were not strong.  God will assist all who are firm in His Cause.
In each cycle the guardians or holy souls have been twelve.  So in the time of Christ there were twelve Apostles.
The superstition about number thirteen originated in the fact that it was the thirteenth disciple, Judas, who betrayed Jesus.  Suppositions regarding lucky or unlucky numbers are imaginary.
Earth's happiness does not depend upon wealth.  The poor depend on God whereas the rich depend on riches.
Christ said so; Baha'u'llah said so...We must be free from the inestimable desire for riches.  The poor are blessed.
The poor are especialy beloved of God.  No deed is greater than helping the poor.  Poverty is a stimulus towards God.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XXI:12, p. 354
lippitt.note: The poor are especially beloved of God.  No greater deed before God than helping the poor.  Poverty is a stimulus towards God.
Jesus referred to Baha'u'llah as the One Who shall come with all His holy angels about Him and all nations gathered before His throne.
Hatred and persecution cannot be reconciled with Christ's teachings.
The stern old law...changed into one of love by the Manifestations.
Consider how much the principles of the religion of Christ have been forgotten...and many heresies appeared.
Explanation of the justice and mercy of God.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:6, p. 104 (*)
lippitt.note: Welcome persecution, hostility and bitterness as the means of testing and increasing your supreme faith in God. (*Words in America)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:18, p. 179 (*)
lippitt.note: The Baha'is must fulfill to the letter the requirements of these holy principles. (*DMAS, 5/23/1914)
allegiance to the Word of God can alone establish unity and love...
Explanation of the justice and mercy of God.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:13, p. 54 (*)
lippitt.note: No connection between the holy teachings and war. (*Answers to questions from theologians in Paris)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, V:8, p. 116
lippitt.note: Remarks about disarmament and Universal Peace.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VI:?, p. 61 (*)
lippitt.note: All religions have two departments--spiritual and material.  The investigation of truth... (*Address)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, II:2, p. 7
lippitt.note: Each one can choose between justice and injustice.  Be just.  Consider the rights of others before your own.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:17, p. 171
lippitt.note: Admonition regarding love.  Love melts all opposition, conquers all foes, sweeps away all barriers; a love that triumphs over all obstacles.

lippitt.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 20 (*)
lippitt.note: Each Prophet left after Him some sign by which He meant some significance.  In this day we are commanded to love mankind. (*Talk by Ibn Abhar on 11/10/1908 in Acca)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VIII:6, p. 68
lippitt.note: By belief we mean that the reality of man will be characterized by divine characteristics.
Every soul is known by his conduct.  Strive to develop a beatific character.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 41
lippitt.note: Those who are born of the Spirit have all the Divine qualities of growth.  Without these qualities they are dead.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 59
lippitt.note: We should give evidence in our actions.  The real quality of man is revealed in his words and deeds.  If a believer shows forth divine qualities we know the true faith is in his heart.
God requires each person to produce something towards the building of the Kingdom of Heaven on earth.  Trees that yield no fruit have been and will ever be for the fire.
His Holiness Jesus Christ summoned all to the Most Great Peace through the acquisition of pure morals.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XV:6, p. 163
lippitt.note: He will seek to be one of the Kingdom...until this station be attained by man his life will be utterly devoid of results.
Therefore we must strive with life and heart that our deeds may be better...cultivate love for all the world.
Thou has said aright that verily 'Abdu'l-Baha looketh to deeds and not to words, even as Christ said.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XV:8, p. 229
lippitt.note: If a believer shows divine qualities we know the true Faith is in his heart.  Three essentials of faith: to confess with the tongue, to believe in the heart, to give evidence in our actions.

Divines' rejection of the Truth fell into depths of ignorance.  How many of the ignorant, by acceptance of the Faith, have soared aloft...

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIII:4, p. 104
lippitt.note: The humbler man is, in the path of God, the more exalted is he.  Every firm believer will consider himself the servant of the friends of God.
Learned men arose against Muhammad while a mere shepherd recognized and embraced Him.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 74
lippitt.note: This is the blessed supper of the Lord...the Heavenly Books prophesy they shall come from the East and the West to sit down in the Kingdom of God.

lippitt.ref: Visit to Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 9

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 3 (*)
lippitt.note: Paul was an enemy of Christ but he became one of His best servants. It is wrong to judge, the end of man is not known. (*Discourse)

Paul in his early life was an enemy of Christ whilst later he became His most faithful servant.  For whether we live, we live unto the Lord, and whether we die, we die unto the Lord.  We can enver know what will be the final judgment.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 3 (*)
lippitt.note: Paul was an enemy of Christ but he became one of His best servants.  It is wrong to judge, the end of man is not known. (*Discourse)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:12, p. 232
lippitt.note: After the baptism of the Holy Spirit, Peter became a great preacher.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:3, p. 39 (*)
lippitt.note: Do not look at present conditions.  The apostles of Christ were insignificant persons, see what took place.  Baha'u'llah's bounties shall in the future be made manifest. (*Tablet to Mrs. Agnes Parsons, 1/8/1919, translated by Shoghi Effendi)
Lowly Peter reached immortal glory while Caiaphas and Annas fell to greatest abasement.
The disciples of Christ engaged in guiding peoples, proved self-sacrificing and suffered martyrdom.
The disciples of Christ were physically weak and apparently vanquished at first--yet in the end they were victorious.

lippitt.ref: My Visit to Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 41
lippitt.note: The Christians refer to the Holy Spirit which came to the disciples after the ascension of Jesus Christ.
In obediance to the command of Jesus the disciples awaited for baptism of the Holy Ghost...
Explanation of descent of Holy Spirit.
Through the power of the Holy Spirit, Peter the simple fisherman was transformed.
through the bounty of the Kingdom he became a great apostle.
I beg God to confirm thee to His will as he confirmed the apostles of Jesus aforetime.
All that hath since been effected...in diffusing fragrances of Christ...hath been the result of the effects produced by that assemblage of disciples.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 96
lippitt.note: Christ taught Peter.  Peter planted the seed.  Through actions qualities express themselves.
The Jews denounced and slew Christ saying, Behold when the Messiah shall come signs and wonders shall testify that He is Christ...they have not appeared.
According to the Jews, Jesus Christ fulfilled none of these conditions therefore they rejected Him.
Observe the gatherings of the disciples after Christ...their trumpets still resounding throughout the ages.
Only communism recommended by the Bab was that of the early Christians, sharing goods in common.
Through Jesus Christ the unchaste and wayward were healed.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VIII:11, p. 144
lippitt.note: The bottle containing the perfumed oil represents the heart of Mary; the ointment, the love of God in Mary's heart...the poor, those who needed love.  We must always cherish the poor for His sake.
No mention of wasting the ointment or of its sale in this Gospel.

lippitt.ref: Visit to Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 42
lippitt.note: In embalming they washed and perfumed the dead.  Christ was to be crucified so she annointed Him and dried it with her hair.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:12, p. 232 (*)
lippitt.note: From Mary Magdalene Jesus Christ cast out seven devils...observe her transformation and glory after she believed in Christ. (*Tablet to Mrs. Annie L Parmerton, Cincinnati, Ohio)
Symbolic of the seed of life which needs experience.
Attainment is dependent upon capacity.  Unless capacity is developed the summons of the Kingdom cannot reach the ear--the light of the Sun of truth will not be observed.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XII:11, p. 182
lippitt.note: Some have no capacity; others hear and understand and forget; others have great capacity and live accordingly.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 43
lippitt.note: All souls have capacity for enkindlement by the Spirit.  The disciples were humble fishermen.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 43
lippitt.note: The seed contained the truth.  Some behold the seed only a hard substance while others see in it the life principle.
He recalled the words of Jesus to His disciples and emphasized the superlative greatness of the New Day. (*the Bab, in His charge to the Letters of the Living)

The Bab admonished the Letters of the Living to ponder the words of Jesus addressed to His disciples as He sent them forth, to shake off the dust from the feet in departing from the city which refused to receive them. (*Bab to Letters of the Living)

Always with their teacher, the divine Master, they learned through His teachings...watching and observing their Master's conduct, followed His example and after His ascension scattered to various regions...we must follow Baha'u'llah

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, V:14, p. 215
lippitt.note: We must act accordingly and follow in their footsteps...
Let them that are men of action follow in their footsteps.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:2, p. 41
lippitt.note: We must be willing to give our lives...we must go among the people of the world...summoning them...
'Abdu'l-Baha's instructions for traveling teachers: travel like 'Abdu'l-Baha, free from every attachment.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:13, p. 122 (*)
lippitt.note: Man must live in the utmost sanctity and purity and he must ever think to assist others and not beg assistance from them. (*DMAS, 10/15/1914, Haifa)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:2, p. 34 (*)
lippitt.note: Teachers must travel to all parts of the world like 'Abdu'l-Baha.  He was sanctified and free from every attachment. (*Tablet unveiling the Divine Plan for the World)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 241 (*)
lippitt.note: The Covenant is like unto the ocean which preserves Baha'i unity...I acted in accordance with Christ's admonition.  (*Tablet to Mr. & Mrs. Howard MacNutt)
They should bear the utmost poverty, seek no reward...Thou shouldst utter with eloquence and joy and guide to manifest light.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, V:1, p. 6
lippitt.note: Command to travel in every country as did the apostles of Jesus Christ.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:14, p. 131
lippitt.note: Consider with what severance, attraction and enkindlement the apostles arose in the promulgation of the Word of God...

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:11, p. 106
lippitt.note: Explanation of the miracle and the symbolism of the loaves and fishes.

lippitt.ref: Visit to Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 34
lippitt.note: Interpretation of loaves--words of Jesus which satisfied the multitude; the fish representing spiritual meaning; remaining basketsful--the superabundance of teaching for others.

lippitt.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 27(*)
lippitt.note: No one is able to follow Christ entirely except he give up all material things such as honour, wealth, etc. (*Talk by Mirza Haydar 'Ali on 11/11/1908)
The land of Syria hath been honoured by the footsteps of its Lord.  The Scriptures have been fulfilled.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 57
lippitt.note: We have said, He who is the Father is come...Blessed is he that draweth nigh unto Him and woe betide them that are far away.

lippitt.ref: The Advent of Divine Justice, p. 65
lippitt.note: Every Prophet hath announced the coming of this Day...allusions were made to it in all the sacred Scriptures.
'Abdu'l-Baha reminds us that we are veiled from the Truth by literal interpretations.

lippitt.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 18
lippitt.note: 'Abdu'l-Baha invites all to partake of the fruit of the Kingdom of the Father, for He discloses mysteries of the teachings of His father, Baha'u'lah. (*Talk by Ibn Abhar, 11/10/1908, Acca)

lippitt.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 19 (*)
lippitt.note: Jesus Christ did not claim to have come in that station but that He would return in the Glory of the Father.  This glory was to be the unity of mankind. (*Talk by Ibn Abhar, 11/10/1908, Acca)

lippitt.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 32 (*)
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah is the Trunk, the branches and the root--all except the fruit, which is 'Abdu'l-Baha.  The teachings of Baha'u'llah have been spread throughout the world. (*Talk by Ibn Abhar, 11/12/1908, Acca)
Jesus Christ referred to His as...Isaiah alluded to Him as the Everlasting Father.
Baha'u'llah arrived in Baghdad.  In it the Tabernacle of the Lord of Hosts was first erected and the foundations of the long-awaited Kingdom of the Father unassailably established.
Jesus Christ returned in the glory of the Father and was about to ascend the throne.
Baha'u'llah announced the advent of One with Whose name the Evangel was adorned.
Baha'u'llah asserts that Jesus Christ was the Herald of His Mission.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:15, p. 258
lippitt.note: The Father, whose coming Jesus Christ foretold, has come among us and opened the Greatest Door of bounteous gifts and Divine favours.
Return of Christ signifies the One to come after Him.  He shall come with the Kingdom of God.

lippitt.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 139
lippitt.note: 'Abdu'l-Baha is not a Manifestation of Godl He is not the Return of Christ, the Son Who will come in the glory of His Father.  He reflects even as a clear mirror the rays of Baha'u'llah's glory.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 61
lippitt.note: Holy Scriptures announce the coming of two Manifestations and these two are the Bab and Baha'u'llah.
The faithful are ever sustained by the presence of the Supreme Concourse.
A spiritual condition expressed as a physical meeting...

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 32
lippitt.note: Proof of the Manifestation is the Manifestation Himself.  Jesus Christ was born in a manger but He changed the whole world by His power and divinity.
God conferred upon that Essence of the Spirit Who was known amongst the people as fatherless, the glory of Prophethood and made Him His testimony unto all that are in heaven and on earth.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 113
lippitt.note: The Sonship of Jesus Christ is asserted by Baha'u'llah.
The Sonship station is the heart of Christ and the Holy Spirit is the station of the Spirit of Christ...meaning that the Sun is visible and manifest in the Mirror.
The disciples of Christ were physically weak and apparently vanquished at first, yet in the end they were victorious.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IX:9, p. 100 (*)
lippitt.note: The humbler a man is in the path of God, the more exalted is he; the greater his meekness and submissiveness, the more beloved is he... (*Talk to believers and students, 7/1914, Haifa)

lippitt.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 27
lippitt.note: Jesus gave His life for mankind.  He proved His own teaching; people believed, then obeyed, then found deliverance from sin.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:9, p. 156 (*)
lippitt.note: All the Prophets appeared that the oneness of man might be taught.  How much suffering these Prophets had to endure to unfold this illumination.  His Holiness offered His life. (*Address in Stuttgart, Germany, 4/6/1913)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:18, p. 179 980
lippitt.note: Let us have the life of the Spirit...of sympathy, practical cooperation, celestial brotherhood... (*DMAS, 5/1914)

It is for this purpose that the Manifestations unveil themselves, bear every ordeal to make ready soul's dawnings of light.  This is the real atonement Christ made...He sacrificed Himself for the world...
So fierce was the opposition to Jesus that He wandered from place to place.  All Manifestations were fiercely opposed.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 49
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah's explanation for Jesus' celibacy given to monks...
He will be seated on the throne of David; behold this man has not as much as a mat whereon to sit...Jews did not see the Reality, the symbol.
Manifestations met with fierce opposition by divines.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 83
lippitt.note: Treatment of Jesus by Jewish leaders analogous to that of Baha'u'llah by divines.
Jesus was an exemplar--detached from all that would bind Him to the material, detached from desire.
Let us compare the lives of some of the Popes with the religion of Christ.  Reflect upon the instructions of Christ and investigate the habits and customs of the Pope.

Be not sorrowful when meeting with worldly difficulties and depressions.  Remember the poverty of Jesus Christ and the Jews' persecution--that poverty was a Light which hath illumined heaven and earth.
Jesus Christ had no home.  He did not sit on the Throne of David, therefore the Jews rejected Him.
Deadness is in the hearts of those who have been deprived of the Holy Spirit.
Without the protection and quickening of the Holy Spirit the human world would be extinguished.
Souls alive physically,  but Christ pronounced them dead in that they were devoid of the spirit of faith.
The alive are those souls in whom the spirit of life is breathed from the Presence of God...all besides them are dead.

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 42
lippitt.note: When souls that are not good die, they go entirely away from this earth and so cannot influence anyone.  They are spiritually dead.
The Spirit of Faith is the flame of reality--cause of eternal illumination.  Though the souls of humanity are living yet if they are deprived of contact with the spirit they are as dead.
Souls deprived of the divine spirit accounted as dead.
Body in need of physical and mental energy but his spirit requires fortification of the Holy Spirit.
Without illumination from God the physicaly alive would be spiritually dead.  Were it not for the love of God the pathway of the Kingdom would be closed.
Those heedless of God are dead compared with the people of faith.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:2, p. 38 (*)
lippitt.note: Whosoever hath not received a portion of the Spirit is as dead. The Spirit is the life of humanity, the cause of eternal illumination. (*Talk, 1/30/1913, rue St. Didier, Paris)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:16, p. 150 (*)
lippitt.note: It is evident that life, in its true sense, is the life of the spirit and that life is the love of God, divine inspiration, spiritual joys and glad tidings of God. (*early Tablet)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVIII:7, p. 224 (*Tablet)
lippitt.note: The body of man is in need of physical and mental energy--the human spirit which is not fortified by the Holy Spirit is dead and in need of resurrection by divine power. (*Tablet)
He would busy these dead was alive with a rational human soul, yet did Christ./..declare such dead; this person was devoid of the Spirit of Faith.
You have found eternal life from among the dead; that is, you have heard the call of the Divine Kingdom...have turned your faces toward the Center of the Light of truth.
Souls who are nt vivified by the Holy Spirit are accounted among the dead...
Those souls who are not vivified and attracted by the Holy Spirit are accounted among the dead.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 41
lippitt.note: Those born of the Spirit have Divine qualities; without these they are dead and are without the power of growth.

lippitt.ref: Visit to Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 18
lippitt.note: Those who lived merely a physical life were to Christ as dead--real life is the life of the Spirit--life eternal.

lippitt.ref: Visit to Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 39
lippitt.note: At death the dust of man is non-existent but in its own station it has existence--the human beings may be spiritually dead bwhile still alive.

lippitt.ref: World Order through World Faith, p. 31
lippitt.note: The soul that is portionless and bereft is dead...the human soul is in need of Divine...vivification emanating from the Holy Spirit.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVI:3, p. 458
lippitt.note: God is absolute love even as His Holiness Jesus Christ declared.  The Prophets have appeared in this world that human souls may attain to love and amity...
This is the original and principal teaching that Jesus Christ spread in the world.
If people of the entire globe are welded into one great commonwealth the prayer, Thy Kingdom come...will be a reality.
Reconstruction of the world must take place before the Will of God can be done on earth...signs of awakening since Baha'u'llah's Revelation.
'Abdu'l-Baha made plans to carry Divine Plan for establishing the Kingdom of God to the Western world.
Prayer is also a prophecy, it depends on you.
Baha'u'llah is the establisher of the Kingdom which Jesus Christ promised.
It is the duty of the followers of Baha'u'llah to proclaim the approach of the Christ-promised Kingdom.
Strive that your ideals may be realized in the Kingdom of God on earth.

This is the glorious time of which the Lord Jesus Christ spoke when He told us to pray, Thy Kingdom come, Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven.  I hope this is your expectation and great desire.
Glory of God eternal...

lippitt.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 46
lippitt.note: A world catastrophe must usher in the Kingdom of the heavenly Father, as anticipated by Jesus Christ.

lippitt.note: Moses announced the coming of Jesus Christ.  Israelites were waiting impatiently for Him but when He came they called Him Beelzebub.
In His day Christ was called Satan, Beelzebub, but hear the bells now ringing for Him!  Although men may rise against the Kingdom, the dominion and sovereignty of God will be set up.
The Jews rejected Jesus and tortured Him but Christianity advanced daily.
The Pharisees called Jesus Beelzebub.  The deriding enemy addeth joy to the heart...becometh the means of spirituality.

lippitt.ref: Utterances in letter from H.E.Hoagg to Mrs. (*)
lippitt.note: When Jesus came the Jews called Him Beelzebub.  I have always said that the believers will be blamed for this Cause.  The Jews proved from the Bible that Jesus was a false prophet.  It is always so. (*I.D. Brittingham, 1/1914, Haifa)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:14, p. 241 (*)
lippitt.note: The Cause of Christ had no importance then.  Importance of the Covenant not yet known befittingly. (*Tablet to Roy C. Wilhelm)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XXV:?, p. 258
lippitt.note: Christ in His day was called Satan, today He is as the Word of God.
Be upon your guard in order that none may assail the oneness and integrity of Baha'u'llah's Cause.

lippitt.ref: The Principle of Religious Unity, p. 2
lippitt.note: when the Jews became fettered by empty forms and imitations, the wrath of God became manifest...
The Jews knew by heart the letter but of the Spirit they understood not a word...they did not understand Scriptures.  Because Jesus dared to rebuke the Pharisees, the Jews rejected Him.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 41
lippitt.note: Analogy between the doctors disputing and rejecting Jesus to the divines rejecting Baha'u'llah.
Beware that thou dispute not with Baha'u'llah even as the Pharisees disputed with Jesus.
Beware that thou dispute not with Baha'u'llah even as the Pharisees with Jesus.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 31
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah warned the Pope, Guard thyself lest darkness spread its veil over thee.  Consider those who opposed Jesus and disputed with Him.
The enemies of His Holiness Jesus Christ were the Jews, the Pharisees, but the enemies of Baha'u'llah were kings representing some 15,000,000 people.  At last His banner was upraised.

lippitt.ref: Utterances in letter of H.E.Hoagg to Mrs. I.D. (*)
lippitt.note: In the time of Jesus the Jewish clergy created so many doubts that there were very few who believed, in reality there were only twelve and one of those denied Him.  Why? Because the tests at that time were very great.
It is the duty of the followers of Baha'u'llah to proclaim the approach of the Christ-promised Kingdom.
Thou wast awake, therefore thou didst advance and engage in the service of the Word of God...
The prophecy in Isaiah 6:9 that He shall be called the prince of peace was not fulfilled in Jesus Christ, therefore the Jews rejected Him.

Certain persons, like the Pharisees...will exert themselves to deprive souls of the glad-tidings of the Holy Spirit.
Treatment of Baha'u'llah analogous to that of Jesus Christ at the hands of ecclesiastical leaders.

Treatment of Baha'u'llah analogous to that of Jesus Christ at the hands of ecclesiastical leaders.
