Analogous to treatment of Baha'u'llah by ecclesiastical leaders.  Adversaries of Jesus Christ were rabbis and their associates ; those of the Bab represented the civil and ecclesiastical authorities who conspired against Him...

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VI:6, p. 43
lippitt.note: The Pharisees held strictly to the law, they were the cause of the condemnation and crucifixion of Jesus.
The betrayal of Baha'u'llah by Mirza Yahya more shameful than the betrayal of Jesus Christ by Judas.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIII:1, p. 21,25
lippitt.note: Judas separated himself from Jesus.  Any person who has left the Cause did so because of his own actions, intrigues and evil plots.  The Tree of life is the Book of the Covenant.
There were people like Judas, who, by their own deeds, separated themselves from Christ...similar to violators who turned against 'Abdu'l-Baha.
Judas chose to turn from Jesus and go his own way.  'Abdu'l-Baha was rejected by the violators.

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 98
lippitt.note: Verily tests withheld a great apostle from the mercy of God and made him take part in the shedding of the blood of Jesus Christ.
Be pure, to be pure is to be selfless.  Jesus had eleven disciples for the twelfth was the cause of His crucifixion.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:14, p. 269 (*)
lippitt.note: Violators like Judas Iscariot have for financial motives deviated from the Covenant.  Baha'i unity cannot be preserved save through the Covenant of God. (*Tablet to Miss Mary Lesch)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:14, p. 270 (*)
lippitt.note: Judas Iscariot first believed in Christ, then turned away from Him.  I hope that thou mayest daily increase in firmness and become the cause of the guidance of other souls. (*Tablet to an American Baha'i)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 242 (*)
lippitt.note: The ocean of the Covenant is eternally surging.  The violators manifest temporary activity but soon, like Judas and his associates and those who approve of his conduct, they shall be completely forgotten (*Tablet to Mr. & Mrs. MacNutt)

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
lippitt.note: Judas was one of the twelve apostles who was not firm.  He betrayed Jesus.  God will assist all those who are firm in His Cause.
Designated as the Lord's supper they ate heavenly food at that supper, the love, knowledge, mysteries and bestowals of God.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IX:2, p. 23(Talk 12/22,Haifa)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:16, p. 269
lippitt.note: It was not in a church.  On that night they had the Last Supper it was in a room.  The idea is the heart should be attracted, the spirit filled with the divine glad tidings and the soul turned to God.  This is the object, other things are secondary.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:16, p. 269
lippitt.note: It is well to worship in any place...the object is to turn to God.  When Christ gathered His disciples together for the Last Supper, it was not a church, it was in a room.  The heart should be attracted, spirit filled with divine glad tidings

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:16, p. 275(Tablet to Mr.Hall)

Whenever such an entertainment is arranged through spiritual sentiments it is the Lord's Supper.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVIII:9, p. 284
lippitt.note: Because there was excessive love among the individual members who had convened there, that table surpassed the royal tables and it was established as the Lord's Supper, even now it is known as such.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 74
lippitt.note: This is the blessed Lord's Supper...for we have gathered under the shadow of the Blessed Perfection...This was indeed a spiritual feast where Love reigned.
The Supper was a spiritual one.  The heavenly food consisted in the love, knowledge, mysteries and bestowal of God.  Religious leaders thought material food was changed into spiritual food.
because there was excessive love amongst them, that table surpassed the royal tables.

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 90 (*)
lippitt.note: It is evident that Christ did not mean material grapes but it was a spiritual state which He interpreted as fruit. (*also in Star of the West, II:15, p. 16)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, II:15, p. 16 (*)
lippitt.note: It is evident that His Highness Christ did not mean material grapes but it was a spiritual condition and a heavenly state which He interpreted as fruit. (*Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 90)
The appearance of the holy Manifestations is for the purpose of causing the heavenly table to descend.  This table means divine virtues and characteristics...
Make an effort in every meeting that the Lord's Supper may become realized and heavenly food...love, affinity, union of souls...then 'Abdu'l-Baha will be present also.
When Christ appeared...He invited men to eternal life; the Light of guidance was shed forth by that radiant Star and He at length gave His life in sacrifice for humanity.
Jesus was not transformed into bread and wine.  They were symbols for bounties.

Christ, who is the Word of God, sacrificed Himself.  This has two meanings, an apparent and an esoteric.  He arose to teach and educate men, He sacrificed Himself to give the spirit of life...to quicken others.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:1, p. 4
lippitt.note: Consider what His Holiness endured, finally sacrificing His own life so that a tent of love and concord might be raised over humanity.

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 21,26
lippitt.note: Christ came of His own will to be a sacrifice that people might become educated and progress.  He saved all who believed on Him.  He wished to become a sacrifice.

These verses have been interpreted by the churches in such a superstitious way that it is impossible for human reason to accpet the explanations...Christ gave His blood in order to guide mankind...He accepted all His trials because of the immortality

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XXII:9, p. 258 (*)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 632

lippitt.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 27 (*)
lippitt.note: There is no love...more perfect than to give one's life...Jesus proved His love...then His followers believed and were delivered from sin. (*Mirza Haydar 'Ali on 11/11/1908, Akka)
If the foot of man deviate as did that of Judas, so that he became human after he had been divine...there is in that a great lesson to people of understanding...

lippitt.ref: Visit to Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 35
lippitt.note: The sword which Jesus meant was the eloquent tongue dividing right and wrong...

lippitt.ref: Visit to Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 35
lippitt.note: Sword is the eloquent tongue described in Isaiah 49:2.  It separates right and wrong as described in Hebrews 4:12.  Peter misunderstood so he was commanded to put his sword into its sheath in John 18:11.
Death of Quddus exceeded the agony of Jesus Christ.

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 98
lippitt.note: Verily, tests withheld a great apostle from the mercy of God, and made him take part in the shedding of the blood of Jesus Christ.  Many a just soul did not endure the power of tests.
Baha'u'llah has left no room for dissension--some wish to create dissension as did Judas.  There should be no association with violators.
Judas turned away from Jesus because of jealousy of Peter.  Consider the predecessors as a warning... and purify thy heart.
Three times Peter denied that he knew Jesus, in a moment of weakness, but he repented and remained firm.

Christ educated eleven; the greatest was Peter who denied Him thrice.  In spite of this the Cause of Christ permeated the world.  Baha'u'llah has educated thousands--reflect what will take place in the future.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:12, p. 207 (*)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IX:2, p. 23(Talk 12/22, Haifa)
lippitt.note: When Jesus Christ was crucified He had twelve disciples...one denied Him...another sold Him for money; notwithstanding this, consider how important was His Cause.  There were at least 100,000 souls who would have sacrificed their lives for Baha'u'llah

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIV:12, p. 353
lippitt.note: Even the glorious Peter was not rescued from the flame of trials--and wavered.
Baha'is must become steadfast...so solid that thousands of deliverances would not cause thee to tremble.
This was before the Holy Spirit descended upon him at Pentecost.
The events connected with Peter and Magdalene was a proof that woman is equivalent and complement of man.
the great one, Peter...denied Him three times; Jews scoffed, persecuted, despised Him...they thought they had quenched the Fire but that Fire burned until it kindled the hearts of the righteous.

lippitt.ref: Tne Days in the Light of Acca, p. 33
lippitt.note: Peter was the greatest of all disciples, yet he denied Jesus Christ three times.  What happened?  The Truth in Christ spread all over the world.
Reflect how the Pharisees persecuted Christ.  The result was that His lamp became ignited; His Light began to shine.
All through life He suffered oppression and hardship.  Humanity was His enemy.  In spite of all this He was the embodiment of pit, supreme goodness and love.

lippitt.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 8 (*)
lippitt.note: Christ said of Himself that He was the Son of God. (*Munirih Khanum on 11/8/1908, Akka)
Through Jesus Christ the leper recieved healing from the leprosy of ignorance.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 27
lippitt.note: The Kingdom of Heaven is within your soul; when the heart is filled with the Light of the Spirit we know that we are in the true heaven.
Learned men arose against Muhammad while a mere shepherd recognized and embraced Him.
The true Baha'i loves the children.  A simple pure heart is near to God.
Blessed are the children of whom His Holiness Christ said, Of such are the Kingdom of heaven.  Hearts must become pure.
As long as you are the children of the Kingdom, no matter what language you use, it is sweet.
Spiritual children are dearer than physical children.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:9, p. 155 (*)
lippitt.note: Because children are tender plants, their hearts are clear and transparent. (*Talk in Stuttgart and Esslingen, Germany--from letters)

Jesus renewed the commandments which Moses had received from Jehovah.  He said the Commandments in the Pentateuch were to be followed and He insisted they must be maintained.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, III:17, p. 8 (*)
lippitt.note: Christ spread the name of Moses throughout the world and promulgated the Old Testament throughout the nations. (*Address in London)
Each soul must devote his life to God's service.
The true seeker will gladly renounce worldly desires and possessions if they interfere with spiritual growth.
Blessed are the poor.  I love you all, you are children of the Kingdom and are accepted of God.
one's heart must not be attached to the things of this world.
The rich depend on riches, whereas the poor are dependent on God.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VIII:2, p. 19 (*)
lippitt.note: Blessed is the rich man whose wealth and oppulence do not prevent him from turning his face towards God, and whose heart is not attached to his possessions.  Such a rich man is the light of the world. (*DMAS, 2/24/1914)

lippitt.note: Jesus Christ manifested Himself in Jerusalem and invited men to eternal life.

The Jews rejected Jesus; they were blind to reality of Christ.  Literal interpretation of prophecies veiled them.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:12, p. 203 (*)

'Abdu'l-Baha uses this incident as an illustration of sedition and Covenant-breaking.  Covenant-breakers are execrated and condemned.

Baha'u'llah identifies Himself with the Promised Father.

lippitt.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 205
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah is in the station of judge...His warning to evil doers.
This is the experience of the Manifestations of God.
The Jews were awaiting the Messiah...when He came Jews rejected Him.  The same in the case of Baha'u'llah.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 81
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah explained that to obey the rulers is to obey God.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 89

Live so that the world may see you loyal to your just government and to God.  This is justice.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 73
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah explained that to obey rulers is to obey God.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 7 (*)

Station of Baha'u'llah identified with that of Moses.
Desolation referred to by Christ ended by the Bab.
The Bab ended this.
We see in these days the fulfilment of divine prophecy.  Jerusalem had disappeared--now it is rebuilt...now its walls and pinacles have been restored and are towering aloft.
Every time He made reply He set forth a special sign that should herald the promised Dispensation.  To this testify the four Gospels.
By the sun in one sense is meant the Suns of Truth Who fill the world with a liberal effusion of grace from on high...the universal Manifestations of God...

Hence it is manifest that by the words the sun shall be darkened and the moon shall not give her light and the stars shall fall is intended the waywardness of the divines and the annulment of laws established by divine Revelation...
Explanations of its symbolism, meanings of sun, heaven, stars, moon, ark, morn...

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 27
lippitt.note: Science proove that nearly all the stars are larger than the earth...the Kingdom of Heaven is within your soul...Symbolism of stars, heaven, etc.

By these words it is meant that when the sun of heavenly teachings hath been eclipsed, stars of divinely established laws have fallen and the moon of knowledge hath been obscured...the sign of the Son of man will appear in heaven.

lippitt.note: Reflect how the Pharisees persecuted Christ.  The result was that His lamp became ignited; His Light began to shine.
The Manifestations met with fierce opposition by the divines.  Reflect how Jesus was treated by His enemies.
Manifestations have been opposed by the embodiment of the powers of darkness.

There was no abuse and persecution which they did not heap upon Him.  The Jews did not investigate reality.  It is incumbent to investigate reality.  Man must examine truth, when he finds it he must hold to it.
All Prophets were scorned and persecuted.  Jesus was rejected, tortured.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 19
lippitt.note: The enemies of Christ persecuted and crucified Him, yet He loved them all.  If the love of God is shining in our hearts we will love all alike.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 31
lippitt.note: All the Prophets of God were scorned and persecuted.  Men have worshipped the personality but were blind to the reality, truth.
The Jews did not know the meaning of the prophecies.  Literal interpretation veiled the true meaning.  They mocked Him, spat in His Holy Face and shed His pure blood.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 81
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah explains that to obey rulers is to obey God.
God, however, aided Him and protected Him...Thy Lord preserveth whom He willeth...

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 100
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah recalled the sufferings of Jesus Christ and old Prophets caused by divines.
Baha'u'llah recalled in anticipation of His own lot the suffering of Jesus Christ.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:9, p. 156 (*)
lippitt.note: He endured all things so that the world might unite and that he might cement the hearts of men through His love. (*Address in Stuttgart, 4/6/1913)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IX:15, p. 171 (*)
lippitt.note: Later on it became manifest that He was the light of truth and was bestowing the spirit of eternal life.  No Prophet cometh unto them but they laugh Him to scorn. (*Tablet to Juanita Storch)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XXII:9, p. 258
lippitt.note: He was mocked, scorned and laughed at yet His Mission was to uplift the very world that refused Him.  Christ was always Christ.
Literal interpretation blinded the eyesight of the Jews and they rejected the One they had been looking for.
The Jews did not understand, therefore they were debarred from perceiving the truth of Christ.

Jews rose against Jesus Christ even to the shedding of His blood.  Had they investigated reality they would have accepted their promised Messiah, Jesus Christ. Blind imitations and hereditary prejudices causes of hatred and war.
Literal interpretation blinded the Jews and prevented them from seeing the Beauty of Christ.  They rejected Him and crucified Him.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 100
lippitt.note: Suffering of Jesus Christ at the hands of the Jews similar to the suffering of other martyrs.
No death of religion founder comparable to that of the Bab except in Gospels
Crucifixion of Jesus Christ compared by Baha'u'llah to the stoning death of Mirza Mihdi.
Jews expected Messiah to be a Prince of Peace; because He did not meet the conditions, they crucified Him.
Christ suffered.  He accepted all His trials because of the immortality of His spirit.

at length He gave His life in sacrifice for humanity...His heart was full of love for all the world, His goodness was destined to reach each one...as they began to realize these things they repented...but He had been crucified.
Christ suffered unto death to bring Light...All prophets of god came to promote unity.
Jesus gave His life that this world might be released from discord and strife.
His Holiness Jesus Christ gave His life upon the cross for the unity of mankind.
may you offer yourselves in the threshold of sacrifice for the betterment of the world.
They did this because they did not investigate truth.
The Talmud and its superstitions veiled His Holiness so completely that they crucified their promised Messiah.
They arose against Him even to the extreme of shedding His blood.
Blind imitations and hereditary prejudices have become the cause of hatred...therefore we must seek truth...
because they held fast to ancestral imitations they were deprived.  Jews crucified Jesus.  Consider how harmful is imitation.  We must investigate truth.
The Jews rejected and tortured Jesus, notwithstanding all Jesus Christ is glorified.  Although men arose against Him and the Kingdom, God's dominion is eternal and is victorious.
All prophets of God were persecuted.  Jesus Christ accepted martyrdom in order to summon mankind to unity and love.  It is the divine purpose of religion.
Jesus Christ, the Bab and Baha'u'llah suffered for mankind because of their love...likewise all the Prophets.
Because they were blindly following imitations, the Jews rejected Jesus.  They refused to investigate truth.
If the Jews had investigated the reality of Christ they would have beheld His truth.  They were withheld because of bigoted imitation.  They were arrogant towards Him and crucified Him.
Comparison of behavior of Peter and Mary Magdalene, a proof that woman is equivalent and a complement of man.

Before the appearance in human form, the Word of God was in the utmost sanctity and glory...when it shone in the body it fell into the hands of the Jews and became the captive of the tyrannical and was crucified.
He arose to teach and educate men and so He sacrificed Himself to give the spirit of life.  He perished in body so as to quicken others by His spirit.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:1, p. 14 (*)
lippitt.note: Remember the most learned doctors of His country, in His age, condemned Him to be killed, whilst one who was a catcher of fishes believed in Him. (*Lawh-i-Aqdas, Baha'u'llah)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:2, p. 40 (*)
lippitt.note: He sacrificed His life that the oil in the lamps of our souls might become ignited and His light shine from them. (*Talk 6/11/1912, New York)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, V:11, p. 169
lippitt.note: They crucified their promised Messiah...

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XV:9, p. 254 (*)
lippitt.note: Jesus Christ gave His life on the cross for the unity of mankind.  Only a few were attracted to Him but with this small army He conquered the world. (*Address)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 633 (*)
lippitt.note: Some Jews cried, O God, hasten the Day when the Promised One will manifest Himself...When He came they knew Him not...they could not see the reality of Christ... (*Bahai Scriptures, p. 729-30)
The body of Jesus Christ was crucified but His Spirit is always pouring upon the world...

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 31
lippitt.note: All Manifestations have been denied and persecuted; men have worshipped the personality but were blind to their Reality...Truth.
The crucifixion was the means of spreading the light of the Cause of God.
In the time of Jesus cruelty and tyranny such that people crucified even Jesus.  Literal interpretation veiled the truth from the Jews.  They had no knowledge of the symbolism of the Scriptures.

lippitt.ref: Utterances in letter of H.E.Hoagg to Mrs. I.D.(*)
lippitt.note: The Jewish clergy were busy creating doubts in the minds of the people...He was crucified.  Therefore they say Jesus was a false prophet. (*Brittingham, 1/1914, Haifa, translated by Monever Khanum)
Thus He pardoned His murderers.  How merciful He was!
Wars are anti-Christ.  A real Christian is to go forth under His banner of peace and love.
We must emulate the Prophets of God.  We must follow Jesus.
Justice and mercy of God explained.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IX:19, p. 222
lippitt.note: Jesus had the power of interceding for the forgiveness of His enemies...By the mercy of God, not through His justice, the condition of those who have died in sin and unbelief can be changed. The power of effective intercession always exists.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 13
lippitt.note: The world must come to know the Word in Christ.  His mission was to uplift the very world which refused Him.
As Christ came from a spiritual heaven of the Divine Kingdom, therefore His disappearance under the earth for three days has an inner signification and is not an outward fact.

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 62
lippitt.note: Because it is in the clear literal text, it is believed...symbolism explained.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:17, p. 307 (Tablet*)
lippitt.note: Laster it became evident what crowns studded with brilliant jewels of guidance were placed on the heads of Mary Magdalene and the other Mary, the Mother of Jesus. (*Unveiling of Divine Plan for the Islands of the Sea, read at 1919 Convention)
Resurrection is symbolic, a spiritual fact.
Mary Magdalene was superior to all the men of her time.  She gathered the others together and said, Why do ye mourn?  Her reality is shining from the horizon of Christ.

lippitt.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 15 (*)
lippitt.note: From Mary Magdalene Jesus cast out seven devils.  Observe her transformation and glory after she believed in Christ. (*Talk 11/10/1908, Akka)
Mary Magdalene strengthened the disciples.  In the sight of God sex makes no difference.  He or she is greatest who is nearest to God.
Comparison between Peter and Mary.  Mary Magdalene an example of the capability of women.  Baha'u'llah emancipated women.
Mary Magdalene was of a lowly type, yet Mary transformed and became the means through which the confirmations of God descended upon the disciples...

Christ had twelve disciples, Judas became a traitor, the remainder were wavering.  It is certain that the one who re-established their faith was Mary Magdalene.  The world consists of two factors, male and female.  Each is the complement of the other.
Mary Magdalene's faith never wavered but the disciples wavered.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
lippitt.note: Mary Magdalene held firmly to the Christ and made others firm.  God will assist all who are firm in His Cause.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:11, p. 108
lippitt.note: Resurrection was a spiritual resurrection.  All should be understood in spiritual terms.  Meaning of symbols...

lippitt.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 28 (*)
lippitt.note: Jesus Christ said, I am the Promised One in your books. (*Mirza Haydar 'Ali, 11/11/1908, Akka)

lippitt.ref: The Principle of Religious Unity, p. 4
lippitt.note: Jesus Christ proclaimed and ratified the prophethood of Moses.
Christ caused the name and prestige of Moses to become widespread.  Through Christ Moses was proclaimed a Prophet of God.
Manifestations testify to oneness, therefore Jesus upheld the teachings and commandments of Moses.  Jews had become blind to the truth through bigoted imitation.
Christ ratified and proclaimed the Law of Moses.  All Prophets have revoiced that same foundation of reality.
Jesus Christ declares Moses to have been a Prophet...through Christ the Old Testament, the Torah, was translated into 600 different tongues and spread throughout the world.

We have stated the valid proofs of the prophethood of Moses.  Jesus Christ was instrumental in publishing the name and greatness of the Israelitish Prophets, and proved to the world that the Israelites constituted the people of God.
Through Jesus the name of Moses was elevated and revered.  He declared Moses to be a Prophet of God.
If the Jews had understood the meanings of the Old Testament they would have become believers of Christ.

Jesus Christ was God's conversation to us, His Revelation.

The Spirit and the Word mean the divine perfections that appeared in the Reality of Christ...the perfections of Christ have the power of the word because a complete meaning can be inferred from a word.

The Word of God from all eternity has always been and will be in the exaltation of santification...
That means it was, is and shall be, for in the world there is no time.
The Holy Manifestations of God are the centers of the Light of Reality...it is said in the Gospel "in the beginning was the Word" meaning that it became the cause of all life.
Explanation of meanings of these words.
Jesus rose, brightening the world until all sects and creeds and nations were taught the beauty of unity.

lippitt.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 113

Baha'u'llah states that Muhammad was a Prophet of God, that Christ was the Word of God and Moses the Friend of God.  Baha'u'llah affirms the principles, spirit and reality of each religion.
Baha'u'llah's words of power were received by some; others despised and rejected Him, as the Jews did Jesus.
Those who believed that Jesus was the Word became disciples.  Faith consisted in blind acceptance of these truths.
The Christ was the Word of God from the beginning.
The Reality of Christ was the embodiment of divine virtues and attributes of God.  He was the Word.  Christ was God made manifest, the conversation of God with man.
The Reality of Christ, the Word of God, is a quickening spirit...
Those who turned towards the Word of God and received His bounties were saved from sin and were delivered from the chains of bondage...
Know that the attributes of perfection, splendor of divine bounties and lights of inspiration are visible and evident in all the Holy Manifestations.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVIII:9, p. 261 (*)
lippitt.note: The glory and exultation of Christ is as clear as the sun at midday from the books and tablets of His Holiness Baha'u'llah. (*Tablet)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XXIII:9, p. 266
lippitt.note: Christ was a quickening spirit and the cause of life in all mankind, the Reality of Christ.
Alas for the souls because of what they have committed against the Word of God, and today they worship the dust which the feet of His beloved have trod.

lippitt.note: Symbolism is water is knowledge, fire is love.  When they were baptized they repented...their hearts were purified and ready to accept the teachings of the One John was declaring would come.  This ceremony has a spiritual meaning but not spiritual effect

Most Great Spirit designated by Baha'u'llah as symbolized in Christian Dispensation by the dove.
The descend of a dove upon Jesus Christ symbolic of the annunciation of the angel to Baha'u'llah regarding His Mission.
This was not a material dove, it was a spiritual state expressed by a figure.
Christ desired this institution to be used at that time...
The Sonship station is the heart of Christ, and the Holy Spirit is the station of the Spirit of Christ.
Jesus was baptized as a man...he came forth with the Manifestation of God in His two-fold nature, the baptism of the Holy Spirit.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIV:9, p. 274
lippitt.note: The story about the dove is simply a metaphor.  No dove came down. The Holy Spirit was always with Christ.  He knew about His Mission from early childhood.
Christ says you must be baptized with the Spirit...This spirit of faith is the flame of reality...cause of eternal illumination.
He who is baptized by the Holy Spirit is a Manifestation of divine mercy to mankind.

Christ appeared in order to illumine the world of humanity...to enkindle the light of love in human souls.  Through Christ they were intended to be baptized by the Holy Spirit, attain a new spirit and realize life everlasting.
Before this the Holy Spirit did not manifest itself in Him.  Humanly the Manifestations are unaware of certain things but their heavenly condition embraces all mysteries...
God conferred upon that Essence of the Spirit, Who was known amongst the people as fatherless, the glory of Prophethood and made Him His testimony unto all that are in heaven and on earth.
The Sonship of Jesus Christ asserted by Baha'u'llah.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 45
lippitt.note: Manifestations are expected but when They come, they are rejected because of the blindness of the people.
Jesus really had only eleven disciples as Judas had an evil heart and betrayed Him.
Consider the days of Christ, how the light of guidance brightened twelve hearts.  The lamp of guidance has been lighted in your hearts.
Only eleven became apostles of Jesus Christ; they illumined the world.  Similar conditions in Paris, little by little the Cause grew.
In each cycle the guardians or holy souls have been twelve.  So in the time of Christ there were twelve apostles.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
lippitt.note: Twelve believed in Him; one deserted Him...others were not strong.  God will assist all who are firm in the Cause.

To be a real Christian is to be a servant in His Cause and Kingdom, to go forth under His banner of peace and love...in all things to be like Him...filled with the spirit of His love.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:1, p. 18
lippitt.note: Christians point out that this was a wonderful event that His Holiness, without opposition from the Jews, was permitted to enter their most sacred place and proclaim the Word of God.

The purport of these words is whosoever is quickened by the breath of the Manifestation...attained unto life, resurrection, entered into paradise...Whosoever is not is condemned to death, deprivation, fire of unbelief and wrath of God.

lippitt.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 37 (*)
lippitt.note: The second birth will be produced and new people will be raised, regenerated. (*Tablet to Miss Alma Knobloch)
The second birth of which Jesus has spoken refers to the appearance of this heavenly nature in man.
Its accomplishment is through the Holy Spirit, through the love of God.
Man must be born again.  Man is a captive of nature until born of the breaths of the Holy Spirit.
so long as he is the captive of nature he is out of touch and without knowledge of the universe of the Kingdom.  Rebirth means his freedom from attachment to mortal and material life.
The human soul is in need of divine vivification emanating from the Holy Spirit--without it man would be an animal.
In this second birth man attains the world of the Kingdom.  This Kingdom is a world of radiance.
That which is born of the spirit is spirit.  This spirit of man...will continue to live forever in the eternal realm.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:12, p. 232 (*)
lippitt.note: This is what is meant by everylasting life, by baptism with the Holy Spirit and by being born again to which Christ refers. (*Tablet to American Baha'i)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:?, p. 284 (Tablet unveiling*)
lippitt.note: The believers of God must become the cause of the establishment of the oneness of humanity...baptizing them with the fire of the love of God...so that the second birth may become realized. (*Divine Plan for the Western World)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:16, p. 269 (*)
lippitt.note: When a man is born, he is freed from material darkness.  In the same way he must be born from the world of nature. This is the second birth...When his spirit overcomes his body the second birth takes place. (*Talk on Mt. Carmel, 9/7)

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 60
lippitt.note: Faith is mentioned in the Scriptures as the second birth.  In this day it is the Spirit of God.
Ye must be born again so that the divine life may spring anew within you.  Look upon other races as sons of the same Father.
Act in such a way that you may find eternal life and be born of water and spirit...need of cleanliness and sanctity.

Christ means that which causes life when He speaks of the water of baptism.  Water and fire are one.  Christ symbolizes that which is the cause of everlasting life when He speaks of water.  Fire is the love of God.

During the time His Holiness Jesus Christ was on earth man sought nearness to God.  Water symbolizes knowledge, fire is the fire of the love of God.  Man must be baptized with these and the Holy Spirit to experience nearness to God...
Paradise and hell of existence found in all the worlds of God., reward and punishment.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, V:19, p. 297
lippitt.note: Answering question of whether all souls enjoy eternal life...

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VI:19, p. 164
lippitt.note: Should partake of eternal life in whom the Spirit of life is breathed from the Presence of God and all besides them are dead, without life.
Explanation of cleanliness and sanctity.  The channel must be cleansed before it is filled with sweet water...

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 41
lippitt.note: Those who are born of the Spirit have all the Divine qualities of growth...otherwise they are dead.

Any person whose insight is opened by God seeth the souls in their station after disintegration of the bodies.

All spiritual prophecies concerning Jesus Christ's coming were fulfilled but Jews shut themselves out.  We are now to dwell...drinking together the water of life from Christ the eternal spring.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 43
lippitt.note: All souls have capacity for enkindlement by the Spirit and we may all be assisted.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 31

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 103

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 35
lippitt.note: I am nothing but the Servant of God.  Some in America are looking for a third Christ or personage.  This is only imagination.  Some call me Christ.  This is also imagination.
The body of Christ was born of Mary of Nazareth, but the Spirit was of God.  The capacities of His human body were limited but the strength of His spirit was vast, infinite, immeasurable...

If they had understood the Reality of Christ, they would have known that the body os His humanity was a cloud that hid His Divinity.  The world only saw His human form, and therefore wondered how He could have come down from heaven.
Jews, knowing that Jesus lived in Nazareth would not accept His statement that He came from heaven.
The Divine Reality of Christ came from heaven but the body was born of Mary.  Literal interpretation veils truth.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIII:5, p. 115
lippitt.note: I will show you the meaning of: His body was born of Mary but His Spirit came from heaven...
all nations were expecting that Christ should come from heaven and He did though outwardly He came from Mary...Now people expect Him to come from heaven.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 32
lippitt.note: He was so poor that He was born in a manger; yet He changed the condition of the whole world.  What proof could be greater than this that He was from God?
Because the people did not know the meaning of heaven they would not believe that He came from heaven.
The product of the life of Christ was the illumined souls of those who believed in Him.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VIII:11, p. 139 (*)
lippitt.note: All the Prophets were sent, all the Books were revealed so that the law of love might be promoted. (*Talk in DMAS, 6/10/1914, in The Divine Art of Living)

lippitt.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 27
lippitt.note: Jesus gave His life for mankind.  He proved His own teaching; people believed then obeyed, then found deliverance from sin.
To Him [Baha'u'llah] Jesus Christ had referred as "The Comforter"..."Spirit of Truth"...
Baha'u'llah identifies Himself with the promised Spirit of Truth...Baha'u'llah asserts that Jesus Christ was the Herald of His Mission.
Baha'u'llah censured the Kings of Christendom for failure to welcome the Spirit of Truth.

lippitt.ref: Visit to Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 39-40
lippitt.note: Christians explain these refer to the Holy Spirit.  There must be a man who can hear and reveal what the Holy Spirit tells him.  Not direct but through an Intermediary, Baha'u'llah.

lippitt.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 105
lippitt.note: I am the One Whose name both the Torah and the Evangel have adorned. (Baha'u'llah quoted)

lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah declared the divines did not know the station of Jesus Christ.
As to the question of the Trinity, that in each one of the cycles there are necessarily three things: Look at the Messianic cycle: the Father and the Son and the Holy Ghost the Intermediary.
Muhammad recognized the truth of the signs, prophecies and words of Jesus and testified that they were all of God.
the Reality of Christ is an unlimited essence.  His Holiness Christ appeared in order to enkindle the light of love in human souls, to unite the hearts...

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 634 (*)
lippitt.note: Those who looked to the spiritual and real existence of the Spirit of Christ firmly believed in Hi8m.  We must not look at the lantern but at the light. (*Bahai Scriptures, p. 959)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XXIII:9, p. 266 (*)
lippitt.note: The position of Christ was that of absolute perfection...the Reality of Christ was a clear mirror of the greatest purity and fineness.  The Essence of Oneness became visible in the mirror. (*Some Answered Questions, p. 136)

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 50
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah declared the divines did not know the station of Jesus Christ.
If they had understood the Reality of Christ they would have known the body of His humanity was a cloud that hid His...

The throne of Jesus Christ was a heavenly throne.  The Jews could not understand this...prophecies concerning Christ were fulfilled but Jews shut their eyes and their ears...the Divine Reality of Christ...unheard, unloved and unrecognized...
His Holiness was heavenly...and belonged to the world of the Kingdom.  He was illumined with love and mercy and so likewise were all the Prophets of God.
Before appearing in human form, the Word of God was in...splendor in the light of its magnificence.  When it shone in the world of the body, it fell into the hands of the Jews and was crucified.

The glorious Word of God, and the Greatest Name Baha'u'llah, are manifestations and evidences beyond imagination.  What a difference between the Word of God and revelations of Isaiah, Jeremiah and Elijah!
The Word of God from all eternity has always been and will be in the exaltation of sanctification.
The Manifestations of universal Prophethood who appeared independently are Abraham, Moses, Christ, Muhammad, the Bab and Baha'u'llah.
for the dominion of the reality of Christ there is no beginning and no end.  This dominion is eternal...has no abrupt end.
When they followed Christ and believed in Him they became spiritual and divine...they became sons of God, they became saints.  Strive to follow in their steps...

browser.central_figures:
Some Answered Questions, Chapter 28, within pp. 116-118
link

lippitt.note: The City [of Certitude] is none other than the Word of God revealed in every age and dispensation; in the days of Moses it was the Pentateuch; in the days of Jesus the Gospel...
All of them were men of the least consequence in the eyes of the world.  But their hearts were pure and attracted by the fires of the divine spirit manifested in Christ.

Therefore it is shown that the Manifestations, the holy mouthpieces of God are the Collective Centers of God.  Collective Centers, Abraham, Moses, Jesus Christ, Muhammad.  Today Baha'u'llah is the Collective Center of unity of all mankind.
Today we behold His manifest signs; His light; His sovereignty is established; His traces are apparent; His bounties are effulgent.
The grand aim of the religion of Christ was to draw the hearts of all men to God's effulgent Truth.
The traces of the Spirit of Jesus Christ, the influence of His Divine Teachings is present with us today and is everlasting.
All the Manifestations of God came with the same purpose and they all sought to lead men into the paths of virtue.  Why do we not love one another and live in unity?
Consider the influence of Jesus Christ on His apostles and their effect upon the world.  These simple men were enabled by the power of the Holy Spirit to spread the glad tidings.
His essential teaching was the unity of mankind and attainment of supreme human virtues through love.

His Holiness Jesus Christ was an educator of humanity...His Teachings were altruistic, universal.  He taught by the power of the Holy Spirit...the essential teachings of Jesus Christ are the light of the world.
His Holiness educated and quickened them with the life of the Word, so they in turn became the instruments of educating the world.  Consider the transformation of Peter.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 32
lippitt.note: The greatest proof of a Manifestation is the Manifestation Himself--God with all His qualities is independent of all His creatures, the sun is independent of Proof.
The reality of Christ was always in heaven.  When we perceive the reality of Christ these symbolic statements become clear.  Establish the fact that the power of God is infinite, unlimited.
These who were followers of Jesus detached their souls from the desires of the world and made their hearts to depend on the bounties of heaven.

Baha'u'llah came to renew the world with this new Springtime.  The world of the Occident should hold fast to the example of Jesus Christ.  Baha'u'llah has refreshed the Orient with His divine springtime in power and glory.
God conferred upon that Essence of the Spirit the glory of Prophethood and made Him His testimony unto all that are in heaven and on earth.

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 22
lippitt.note: meaning that the qualities of the Father were in Him and His qualities were in them...
The Messianic reality is like unto a mirror through which the Sun of Divinity has become resplendent.  This is symbolical.
The image of the Sun of Reality appears in the mirrors of the Manifestations of God.
Explanation of the three stations of Manifestations: physical, rational soul, divine appearance.
This is an intellectual state which is expressed in a sensible figure.
Explanation of this verse and of the Trinity.
The Holy Reality of the Word of God is in the condition of the pure, fine, shining mirror reflecting perfection.
If the divine power in man, which is his essential perfection, overcomes the satanic power, he becomes the most excellent among the creatures...

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:3, p. 52 (*)
lippitt.note: This is symbolical and allegorical... (*Answers to questions from theologians in Paris)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, VII:?, p. 161 (*)
lippitt.note: Man is enabled to discover reality and bring the invisible things into the visible... (*Address at Green Acre, 8/20/1912)
The Christ Reality was like a pure mirror and the Sun of Reality shone upon it from the Holy Horizon.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, III:3, p. 6 (*)
lippitt.note: Christ came into the world that people might have, through Him a spirit of oneness with God.  There must be a oneness in the world of humanity, then will we have the Most Great Peace. (*First public address in USA)

lippitt.ref: STar of the West, XVI:3, p. 458
lippitt.note: God is absolute love even as His Holiness Jesus Christ declared.  The Prophets have appeared in this world that human souls may attain to love and amity.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XXII:9, p. 258
lippitt.note: Consider the essential teachings of His Holiness Jesus Christ...they are the very source of life...the cause of happiness.
Christ established the religion of God through love.  His sovereignty is everlasting.  Napoleon overthrew governments in war and bloodshed...his dominion passed away...
Judas chose to turn from Christ and go his own way.  'Abdu'l-Baha rejected by the violators.  No one must associate with them.
Be pure, to be pure is to be selfless...My hope is that you may become as Mary Magdalene.  Judas was the cause of the crucifixion, Peter's fatih was shaken, Mary Magdalene was a veritable lioness.

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 98
lippitt.note: Verily, tests withheld a great Apostle from the mercy of God, and made him take part in the shedding of the blood of Jesus Christ.
The betrayal of Baha'u'llah by Mirza Yahya more shameful than the betrayal of Jesus Christ by Jesus.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 3 (*)
lippitt.note: Judas began well but failed in the end...It is wrong to judge, the end of man is not known.  (*Discourses in Paris and London)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 242 (*)
lippitt.note: The Covenant is the ocean, violators are the foam...The violators manifest temporary activity but soon, like Judas and his associates shall be completely forgotten. (*Tablet to Mr.&Mrs. Howard MacNutt, Brooklyn)

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
lippitt.note: Judas, one of the twelve disciples weakened and betrayed Jesus Christ.  God will assist all those who are firm in the Cause.
There were people like Judas, who, by their own deeds, separated themselves from Christ [like violators who separated themselves from 'Abdu'l-Baha].
Baha'u'llah has left no room for dissension.  Some wish to create dissension as did Judas.  Violators must be sent away.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIII:1, p. 21,25
lippitt.note: Judas separated himself from Jesus.  Any person who has left the Cause did so because of his own actions, intrigues and evil plots.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:14, p. 270 (*)
lippitt.note: Judas Iscariot first believed in Christ, then turned away from Him.  I hope that thou mayest daily increase in firmness and become the cause of guidance of other souls.  (*Tablet to Miss Mary Hall, Chicago)
Judas turned away from Jesus Christ because of jealousy of Peter.  Consider the predecessors as a warning and purify thy heart.
If the foot of man deviate as did that of Judas, so that he became human after he had been divine, there is in that a great lesson to people of understanding.
Impossible to train another until he first conquers his own self and his desires...Judas was deprived of the Kingdom of God.
Jesus Christ forbade warfare.
Christ taught, resist not evil.  Differences will disappear when we hold fast to the foundation of religion.
Christ said to Peter, Put up thy sword into the sheath.  Yet in spite of the direct command of the Lord they profess to serve, men make war.  His counsels seem quite forgotten.
The purpose of the life of Jesus Christ and the glory of His death was to set mankind free from the sin of strife, war and bloodshed...These wars are anti-Christ.
The meaning is that warfare is forbidden and abrogated.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:3, p. 55 (*)
lippitt.note: When we hold fast to the foundations of the religions of God, all differences are dispelled. (*Answers to questions from theologians in Paris)

lippitt.note: Each Manifestation has been opposed by the embodiments of the powers of darkness.
They were collosal pillars of the Moses Dispensation...but as they did not acknowledge the Word of God, they fell from the apex of glory to greatest abasement.

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 48
lippitt.note: Analogy between the doctors who disputed with Jesus and the divines rejecting Baha'u'llah.

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 98
lippitt.note: The tests made Peter, the apostle, deny Christ.  Many a just soul did not endure the power of tests.
The leader of the apostles was Peter.  On the night of Christ's crucifixion his faith was shattered, thrice he denied Christ.  Afterwards he became firm.
This was before the Holy Spirit had descended on him at Pentecost.
Comparison of the behavior of Peter and that of Mary Magdalene at the time of the crucifixion of Jesus Christ...a proof that woman is equivalent and a complement of man.

Christ only educated eleven men; the greatest of these was Peter who, when tested, thrice denied Christ.  In spite of this the Cause of Christ permeated the world.  Reflect what will take place in the future.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:12, p. 207 (*)
lippitt.note: Comparison made between Peter and Mary Magdalene... (*Interivew with San Francisco newspaper correspondent, 10/3/1912)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IX:2, p. 23 (*)
lippitt.note: Wehn Jesus Christ was crucified He had twelve disciples, one denied Him and another sold Him for money...There were at least 100,000 souls who would sacrifice their lives for Baha'u'llah at the time of His departure. (*Talk in DMAS, 12/22, Haifa)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XIV:12, p. 353 (*)
lippitt.note: Even the glorious Peter was not rescued from the flames of trials...and he wavered. (*Tablet to Mrs. I.D. Brittingham)
Peter who was the Rock was firm and solid...but the Rock quaked a little.  I hope you will become steadfast, firm, solid.
the great one Peter...denied Him three times...

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 33
lippitt.note: Peter was the greatest of the disciples yet he denied Jesus three times.  What happened?  The Truth in Christ spread all over the world.  The kings of the earth cannot stand against the Word of God.

lippitt.note: The most learned of His age pronounced judgment against Jesus, while fishermen believed in Him.  Take heed, men of understanding!

lippitt.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 100
lippitt.note: Baha'u'llah recalled the sufferings of the Prophets at the hands of divines similar to those of Jesus Christ.
The Pharisees persecuted Jesus Christ.  The result was that His lamp became ignited, His light began to shine...
the Manifestations met with fierce opposition by the divines.  Reflect how Jesus was treated by His enemies.
Baha'u'llah recalled in anticipation of His own lot that of Jesus Christ.
Manifestations have been opposed by the embodiment of the powers of darkness.
all through His life He suffered persecution and hardship.  Humanity was His enemy.  In spite of all this He was the embodiment of pity, supreme goodness and love.

There was no abuse and persecution which they did not heap upon Him.  The Jews did not investigate truth.   It is incumbent to investigate reality.  Man must examine truth, when he finds it he must hold to it.
The Jews rejected Jesus Christ--tortured and crucified Him.  All prophets were persecuted.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:9, p. 156 (*)
lippitt.note: He endured all things so that the world might unite and that He might cement the hearts of men through His love... (*Address in Stuttgart, 4/6/1913)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IX:15, p. 171 (*)
lippitt.note: Later on it became manifest that He was the light of truth and was bestowing the spirit of eternal life.  No Prophet cometh unto them but they laugh Him to scorn. (*Tablet to Juanita Storch)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XXII:9, p. 258
lippitt.note: He was mocked, scorned and laughed at, yet His Mission was to uplift the very world that refused Him.  Christ is always Christ.
Remember what the Pharisees published concerning Jesus.  Of the same class are the Pharisees and the priests in this manifest Day.  They are isolated from the Lord's gifts, deaf, dumb and blind.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 13
lippitt.note: The world must come to know the Word in Christ.  His mission was to uplift the very world which refused Him.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 31
lippitt.note: All Prophets of God were scorned and persecuted.  Men have worshipped the personality but were blind to the Reality, Truth.
The Jews did not know the meaning of the prophecies.  Literal interpretation veiled the true meaning.
Jesus Christ knew that all earthly diadems were at His feet, therefore He said, All power is given Me, in heaven and on earth.
His abasement was His glorification; His crown of thorns is a heavenly diadem.  When they pressed it upon His blessed head...they laid the foundation of His everlasting kingdom.
To all outward appearances they overcame Him...but all the kings of the earth have not been able to withstand Him.

Literal interpretation veiled the truth of Jesus from the Jews, though they had been looking for the Messiah...Those who had clear insight realized the Messiah as the rising Sun...the thorns as a brilliant diadem...
Of the same class are the Pharisees and priests in this manifest Day, they are isolated from the gifts of your Lord.
Jesus wore a diadem of thorns...now people adorn their heads in His Name with most brilliant gems.  Woe unto such heedless people!
Analogous to treatment of the Bab by religious leaders.
Analogous to treatment of Baha'u'llah by ecclesiastical leaders.
Notwithstanding the fulfillment of all prophetic signs in Jesus, the Jews denied Him--because of their allegiance to literal and ancestral teachings.
Even the crucifixion did not prevent the spread of the Gospel.  Nothing can frustrate the will of God.
The scribes and Pharisees spread slanders and caused Him to be crucified...
Before the appearance in human form, the Word of God was in the utmost sanctity and glory...when it shone in the body it fell into the hands of the Jews and was at last crucified...

lippitt.ref: Utterances in letter of H.E.Hoagg to Mrs. I.D.(*)
lippitt.note: The Jewish clergy...said in the Day of the Kingdom even the animals would live in peace but when Jesus came the oppression and cruelty were so great that He was crucified.  Therefore they say Jesus was a false Messiah.  Those who have weak faith...
Jews rose against Jesus Christ even to shedding His blood.  Had they investigated Reality they would have accepted their promised Messiah, Jesus Christ.
Literal interpretation blinded the Jews and prevented them from seeing the Beauty of Christ.  They rejected and crucified Him.
No death of a religion-founder comparable to that of the Bab except in the Gospels.
Crucifixion of Jesus Christ compared by Baha'u'llah to the atoning death of Mirza Mihdi, the intended sacrifice of Abraham and the martyrdom of the Imam Husayn.
Jews expected Messiah to be a Prince of Peace.  Because He did not meet the conditions they crucified Him.
Christ suffered.  He accepted all His trials because of the Immortality of His Spirit.
Christ suffered unto death to bring Light...All Prophets of God came to bring oneness of mankind.
They did this because they did not investigate the truth.
He arose to teach and educate men so He sacrificed Himself to give the spirit of life.  He perished in body so as to quicken others by His Spirit.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:2, p. 40 (*)
lippitt.note: He sacrificed His life that the oil in the lamps of our souls might become ignited and His light shine from them... (*Address in New York, 6/11/1912)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, V:11, p. 169
lippitt.note: They crucified their Promised Messiah...
The scribes and Pharisees spread slanders and caused Him to be crucified.  They conspired to make the people believe Him to be a liar.
Jesus gave His life that this human world might be released from discord...
may you offer yourselves in the threshold of sacrifice for the betterment of the world.
The Talmud and its superstitions veiled His Holiness so completely that they crucified their promised Messiah.
Blind imitations and hereditary prejudices have become the cause of hatred, therefore we must seek truth.  They rose against Him even to the shedding of His blood.
The Jews crucified Jesus...because they held fast to ancestral imitations, they were deprived.  It is evident we must forsake all such imitations and beliefs.
Jews rejected and tortured Jesus and crucified Him, but Jesus Christ was glorified...
Jesus Christ, the Bab, and Baha'u'llah suffered for mankind because of their love.
They sentenced Him to death...Because they were blindly following imitations, the Jews rejected Jesus.  They refused to investigate truth.
If the Jews had investigated the reality of Christ, they would have beheld His truth.  They were withheld because of bigoted imitation.
Comparison between behavior of Peter and of Mary Magdalene at time of the crucifixion of Jesus.
Christ offered His life as a sacrifice in order to illumine humanity....accepted calamity and death in His laws for man.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, IV:1, p. 14 (*)
lippitt.note: Remember the most learned doctors of His country, in His age, condemned Him to be killed, whilst one who was a catcher of fishes believed in Him. (*Lawh-i-Aqdas, Baha'u'llah)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XV:9, p. 254 (*)
lippitt.note: Jesus Christ gave His life on the cross for the unity of mankind.  Only a few were attracted to Him but with this small army He conquered the world. (*Address in New York)

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 633 (*)
lippitt.note: Some Jews cried, O God, hasten the Day when the Promised One will manifest Himself.  When He came they knew Him not...they killed Him... (*Baha'i Scriptures, p. 729-30)
The body of Jesus was crucified but His Spirit is always pouring upon the world.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 31
lippitt.note: All Manifestations have been denied and persecuted; men have worshipped the personality but were blind to the Reality.
The crucifixion was the means of spreading the light of the Cause of God.

Literal interpretation veiled the truth from the Jews.  They had no knowledge of the spiritual symbolism of the Scriptures.  In the time of Jesus cruelty and tyranny were such that people crucified Jesus.
His Holiness Jesus Christ gave His life upon the cross for the unity of mankind.

lippitt.ref: Some Answered Questions, chapter XXIII, p. 120
lippitt.note: Resurrection is symbolic, a spiritual fact, not physical.

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 62
lippitt.note: Because it is the clear literal text, it is believed in by many without any explanation.

lippitt.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 15
lippitt.note: Jesus cast seven devils from Mary Magdalene.  She was transformed and became one of his faithful disciples.
Mary Magdalene was superior to all the men of her time.  She gathered the others together and said, Why do ye mourn?  Her reality is shining from the horizon of Christ.
Comparison between Mary Magdalene and Peter and example of the capability of woman.  Baha'u'llah emancipated woman.
Mary Magdalene was transformed and became the means through which the confirmation of God descended upon the disciples.

lippitt.ref: Star of the West, X:17, p. 307 (*)
lippitt.note: Later on it became evident what crowns studded with brilliant jewels of guidance were placed on the heads of Mary Magdalene and the other Mary, the Mother of Jesus. (*Unveiling of Divine Plan for islands of the sea, read 1919)
Mary Magdalene's faith never wavered, honored with the commission to tell the disciples.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
lippitt.note: Mary Magdalene held firmly to the Christ and made others firm.  God will assist all those who are firm in His Cause.

lippitt.note: Through the power of the Holy Spirit these simple fishermen were able to do what philosophers and scientists failed to accomplish.

lippitt.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 74
lippitt.note: Believers from all parts of the East at a Feast under the shadow of the Blessed Perfection.  This is the blessed Supper of the Lord, We are the lambs of the Blessed Perfection...
By the power of the Holy Spirit S. Peter and St. John the Evangelist who were once simple workmen were illumined by the Holy Spirit and they received eternal blessings of the Lord Christ.
There is a difference among believers as to the second coming of Christ, the Promised Christ is Baha'u'llah and the Bab.
As disciples of Jesus observed His teachings and example and carried the Faith, so should the followers of Baha'u'llah also do.

lippitt.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 62
lippitt.note: Because it is the clear literal text many still believe this, without any explanation.
After Jesus Christ's ascension the Cause grew little by little.  This is a promise for the future.
Ascension is symbolic, a spiritual fact.  This subject of the ascension of Christ with an elemental body to the visible heaven is contrary to the science of mathematics...
We are veiled from the truth by literal interpretations...humanity has wandered far from the true teachings of God.  Christians believe that the Promised One will descend from the sky.
When Christ appeared He came from heaven although outwardly He was born of Mary.
The heaven whither Christ ascended is not an infinite space.  His heaven is the Kingdom of His Lord.

lippitt.note: Day of Baha'u'llah described as the time of refreshing.
He came, but the Jews are still waiting.
Only communism recommended by the Bab was that of the early Christians, sharing goods in common.

lippitt.ref: Paper by Shahnaz Waite (out of print #20)
lippitt.note: The first Baha'i principle is the oneness of the world of humanity.  The first foundation stone is the jasper, it is clear red, like the hue of the heart.

lippitt.ref: Paper of Shahnaz Waite (out of print #20)
lippitt.note: The second Baha'i basic principle, the independent investigation of truth.  All must be free to seek truth in their own way.  Saphire is clear blue, the color of faith, inspiration, loyalty, truth.  Man must be free to soar in the dome of heaven's bl

lippitt.ref: Paper by Shahnaz Waite (out of print #20)
lippitt.note: The third Baha'i basic principle, the foundation of religion is one...chalcedony is pure white...white is apparent, yet in it are hidden seven colors, white is the symbol of purity...spiritual symbols and not material stones...

lippitt.ref: Paper by Shahnaz Waite (out of print #20)
lippitt.note: The fourth Baha'i basic principle is religion must be the source of unity...fourth stone is emerald, green, the color of harmony and unity...no color known that green will not harmonize with, color of immortality and humility.

lippitt.ref: Paper by Shahnaz Waite (out of print #20)
lippitt.note: The fifth Baha'i basic principle is science and religion must agree, neither must deny the other...sardonyx is red or brown according to the way it is held...religion and science are in essence one but different in color of manifestation.

lippitt.ref: Paper by Shahnaz Waite (out of print #20)
lippitt.note: The sixth Baha'i basic principle, the equality of men and women, they must be as two wings of the soul each equally developed...sardius is a stone of two layers, one white, one red, symbolic of the two in one.

lippitt.ref: Paper by Shahnaz Waite (out of print #20)
lippitt.note: The seventh Baha'i basic principle is removal of all prejudice, religious, racial and all superstitions; chrysolyte is exquisitely clear green, symbol of absolute sincerity and truthfulness, purity and selflessness.

lippitt.ref: Paper by Shahnaz Waite (out of print #20)
lippitt.note: The eighth Baha'i basic principle is universal peace...beryl is bluish green, blue of faith and loyalty toward our brother man, the green of humility and unity; harmonious blending into one symmetrical whole like a beautiful bouquet of flowers...

lippitt.ref: Paper by Shahnaz Waite (out of print #20)
lippitt.note: The ninth Baha'i basic principle is universal education...the topaz, the glory of the sun; symbolic of the light of knowledge and the glory of wisdom which is universal education established in the world...

lippitt.ref: Paper by Shahnaz Waite (out of print #20)
lippitt.note: The tenth Baha'i basic principle, solution of the economic problem, the chrysoprasus is like chrysolyte a clear green which embodies truthfulness, justice, sincerity and purity, and these are the requisites necessary...the Golden Rule.

lippitt.ref: Paper by Shahnaz Waite (out of print #20)
lippitt.note: The eleventh Baha'i basic principle, a universal auxiliary language...jacinth is like the jasper, a clear red, the universal color, the hue of the heart, can only understand the heart of another when it can speak the same language.

lippitt.ref: Paper by Shahnaz Waite (out of print #20)
lippitt.note: The twelfth Baha'i basic principle, the world tribunal or parliament of man...amethyst is violet, symbolizes spiritual light, reverence, healing, serenity, poise.  This tribunal...must be as the ultra-violet rays for the healing of the nations

lippitt.ref: Paper by Shahnaz Waite (out of print #20)
lippitt.note: There are twelve Baha'i basic principles upon which this New Jerusalem rests, which are most remarkably co-related to twelve precious stones, spiritual symbols and not material stones both in significance and color...
St. John alluded to Baha'u'llah as the Glory of God.

lippitt.ref: Paper by Shahnaz Waite (out of print #20)
lippitt.note: In each heart must this Holy City be built, that each heart may become one of the precious stones of which the universal city shall be constructed.  Upon the twelve basic principles will it rest....must be studied until they are realized...
